description:
About the job

Job Title: Conversational AI Engineer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

description:
About the job

Business Developer Executive | DICKA Logistics




En DICKA Logistics nos encontramos en búsqueda de talento para integrarse a nuestro equipo comercial como Business Developer Executive.




Buscamos una persona con experiencia en el sector logístico, preferentemente en empresas 3PL, transporte, distribución, freight forwarding o soluciones integrales de cadena de suministro.




La posición requiere conocimiento del proceso comercial end to end, desde la prospección de nuevos clientes hasta el cierre de oportunidades, asegurando una adecuada identificación de necesidades, construcción de propuestas, análisis de viabilidad operativa y seguimiento puntual de cada oportunidad comercial.




Principales responsabilidades:
Prospectar y desarrollar nuevas oportunidades de negocio en el sector logístico.
Identificar necesidades de clientes en servicios 3PL, transporte, distribución, almacenaje o soluciones logísticas integrales.
Gestionar el ciclo completo de venta: prospección, diagnóstico, propuesta, negociación y cierre.
Elaborar, analizar y dar seguimiento a cotizaciones comerciales.
Participar en procesos de pricing, análisis de tarifas, costos y márgenes.
Coordinarse con áreas operativas para asegurar que las soluciones ofrecidas sean viables y rentables.
Dar seguimiento a clientes actuales y potenciales, fortaleciendo relaciones comerciales de largo plazo.
Asegurar una correcta transición entre la oportunidad comercial cerrada y la ejecución operativa del servicio.




Perfil requerido:
Experiencia en ventas B2B dentro del sector logístico.
Conocimiento en empresas 3PL, transporte, distribución o freight forwarding.
Experiencia en prospección, negociación, cotización y cierre comercial.
Entendimiento operativo de transporte terrestre, almacenaje, distribución o soluciones logísticas.
Capacidad de análisis para evaluar tarifas, costos, márgenes y rentabilidad.
Orientación a resultados, seguimiento estructurado y enfoque al cliente.




En logística, una venta no termina con una firma: comienza cuando la promesa comercial se convierte en una operación cumplida.




Buscamos a una persona con visión comercial, sensibilidad operativa y capacidad para construir relaciones de negocio sostenibles.




Las personas interesadas pueden postularse o compartir esta publicación con perfiles alineados a la posición.

#DICKALogistics #BusinessDeveloper #BusinessDevelopment #VentasB2B #Logística #3PL #Transporte #SupplyChain #Pricing #DesarrolloDeNegocios #TalentoComercial #Hiring

title: Partnerships Associate
description:
About the job

Hiring: Partnerships Associate for Million Dollar Sellers, a top-tier ecommerce community.




You’ll own the full partner lifecycle, close new partners, then make sure every commitment runs cleanly.


ABOUT MDS

An exclusive community of 700+ seven- and eight-figure Amazon and ecommerce founders. $12B+ in combined member sales. Nearly 100 events per year. Backed by industry-leading partners.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

— Identify and close new partners across affiliates, event sponsorships, and community offerings, owning outreach from first contact to signed agreement

— Onboard every new partner completely into the database, app listing, newsletter, offers, and deliverable tracker before anyone has to ask

— Track and execute all partner deliverables across events, campaigns, and affiliate programs so nothing promised slips

— Send the weekly Partners Newsletter every Tuesday and maintain the partner database, contracts, offers, and KPI tracking

— Produce AI-powered Community Pulse summaries surfacing partner mentions and insights from across community channels


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

— Experience in partnerships, sales, or BD, with a track record of closing deals, not just managing them

— Equally comfortable sending a cold outreach email and cleaning up a database

— Operational discipline: you onboard fully, track deliverables proactively, and treat the database as a live system, not an archive

— Familiarity with CRMs and ops tools, Airtable, ClickUp, SignNow, or similar

— Comfort using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) as part of your regular workflow

— Strong written English, you’ll be writing outreach, a weekly newsletter, and partner comms

— Based in LATAM (Mexico, Argentina preferred). Travel required minimum 3x per year

— Available 10AM–3PM EST core hours




COMPENSATION

— USD $2,000–$3,000/month (band reflects balance of sales track record and operational depth)

— Tiered bonus plan tied to annual revenue goals

— Long-term, multi-year engagement

— Direct line to the VP of Revenue




HOW TO APPLY

Apply through our form: https://forms.clickup.com/90161589637/f/2kz0tbc5-516/HN6BGAJ1CDZMH3QDD7




You’ll need: CV, a Loom intro, and 5 minutes.

We respond to every applicant within 48 hours.

title: Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d)
skills: Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.
Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.
Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.
description:
About the job

At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d) to join our North America commercial team. This is not a purely analytical role — we need someone who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to design and implement solutions, build bridges across stakeholders, and take ownership of results. You will interact directly with senior leadership, including the CEO for North America, and shape the commercial strategy of one of the world's fastest-growing mobility networks.

About The Role


Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.


About You


Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

description:

About Forceget

Forceget is a tech-enabled global logistics and fulfillment company powering ecommerce sellers across Amazon, Shopify, Walmart, and DTC channels. We offer international shipping, warehousing, FBA prep, seller reimbursements, and profit optimization tools that help brands scale without the operational headaches.




The Role

We're hiring a Business Development Representative to drive outbound pipeline for our sales team. You'll prospect, qualify, and book meetings through multi-channel outreach — cold calls, email, WhatsApp, and LinkedIn — using proven playbooks designed to get results. This is a high-activity, high-accountability role for someone who wants to compete, hit targets, and grow fast.




What You'll Do

Complete structured onboarding and ongoing training through our internal LMS

Proven track record of hitting activity and pipeline targets
Comfortable with high call volumes and handling rejection

Self-disciplined, punctual, and accountable without needing oversight

Structured onboarding with dedicated training materials and playbooks

A fast-paced, results-driven team that rewards hustle and ownership

title: Growth Marketer
skills: Nurture and conversion. Build and optimize nurture, re-engagement, and sales-funnel catalyst campaigns in GoHighLevel that move new leads through the funnel and into sales conversations.
Lead-to-sales handoff. Design the handoff so reps get context, not just a name. The goal is qualified leads that are ready to engage, not raw volume.
Paid search. Improve performance from Google Ads, from targeting and copy to landing pages and conversion tracking. Kill what’s not working, double down on what is.
Social creative. Manage the generation and optimization of creative for social ads, often AI-assisted. Test fast, read the data, cut losers quickly.
Reporting. Work with Operations to build improved reporting across all leads and the full marketing funnel, from first-touch attribution through to closed revenue.
The number. Own a monthly qualified-lead and pipeline target. You’ll have visibility into your performance and the autonomy to act on it.
Creative. You come up with the hook, the angle, and the test, not just the spreadsheet.
AI-native. You use AI well beyond chat. You’ve built loops, agents, or automations to do your own job faster, and you can show us what you’ve made.
A systems thinker. Your instinct is to build a repeatable system, not to grind through the same manual task twice.
Metrics-driven. You’re comfortable owning a number and being measured against it. You speak in CPL, CAC, conversion rate, and ROAS.
Excited by speed. You want to work at a fast-growing company where you ship, learn, and iterate, not wait on a quarterly planning cycle.
Target-based Experimenter: testing > assuming. You build and measure, adjust, repeat, and then scale. And know when to double down based on data + your experience.
Your answer to every lead-gen problem is “spend more on ads.” Paid is one channel, not the strategy.
You avoid committing to a number, or treat activity as the same thing as results.
description:
About the job

Growth Marketer

Seneca Cost Segregation · Remote · Salary: $2000-$3000/month + Target based bounus




About Seneca

We help real estate investors keep more of their money. Cost segregation is a tax strategy that accelerates depreciation and puts cash back in our clients’ hands, often six figures in Year 1. We’ve performed over 10,200 studies and analyzed more than $1B in cost basis, and we’ve never lost an IRS audit.

We’re a lean, fast-growing team scaling toward $5–10M. That means real ownership, real budget, and real impact from day one. No layers, no politics, no waiting for permission.




The Role

We’re hiring a Growth Marketer to own the full marketing funnel, from first touch to qualified pipeline. You’ll build the nurture and conversion systems that turn leads into sales conversations, sharpen our paid channels, and create the reporting that tells us exactly what’s working.




This is a hands-on builder role, not a team-management role (yet). You’ll do the work, with the leverage to grow as we scale. If you think in systems instead of manual effort, use AI to multiply your own output, and want to see your work move the revenue number, this is for you.




What You’ll Own

Nurture and conversion. Build and optimize nurture, re-engagement, and sales-funnel catalyst campaigns in GoHighLevel that move new leads through the funnel and into sales conversations.
Lead-to-sales handoff. Design the handoff so reps get context, not just a name. The goal is qualified leads that are ready to engage, not raw volume.
Paid search. Improve performance from Google Ads, from targeting and copy to landing pages and conversion tracking. Kill what’s not working, double down on what is.
Social creative. Manage the generation and optimization of creative for social ads, often AI-assisted. Test fast, read the data, cut losers quickly.
Reporting. Work with Operations to build improved reporting across all leads and the full marketing funnel, from first-touch attribution through to closed revenue.
The number. Own a monthly qualified-lead and pipeline target. You’ll have visibility into your performance and the autonomy to act on it.




Who You Are

Creative. You come up with the hook, the angle, and the test, not just the spreadsheet.
AI-native. You use AI well beyond chat. You’ve built loops, agents, or automations to do your own job faster, and you can show us what you’ve made.
A systems thinker. Your instinct is to build a repeatable system, not to grind through the same manual task twice.
Metrics-driven. You’re comfortable owning a number and being measured against it. You speak in CPL, CAC, conversion rate, and ROAS.
Excited by speed. You want to work at a fast-growing company where you ship, learn, and iterate, not wait on a quarterly planning cycle.
Target-based Experimenter: testing > assuming. You build and measure, adjust, repeat, and then scale. And know when to double down based on data + your experience. 

 

This Probably Isn’t a Fit If…

Your answer to every lead-gen problem is “spend more on ads.” Paid is one channel, not the strategy.
You avoid committing to a number, or treat activity as the same thing as results.




Our Stack

GoHighLevel (CRM and automation), Google Ads, Meta Ads, GA4 and Google Search Console, GTM, WordPress site, Claude AI and some Codex. You don’t need to be an expert in all of these on day one, but you should be the kind of person who gets fluent in a new tool fast and self-starts to hit your targets.




How to Apply

Send us a short note on a growth system you built and the result it drove. Include one example of a loop, agent, or automation you made to speed up your own work. We read every application that shows us something real.

title: B2B Marketing Consultant
description:
About the job

IMPORTANT - Watch this quick Loom video on how to get hired:

https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED, THE SELECTED CANDIDATE WILL BE SPEAKING STRICTLY IN ENGLISH DURING THEIR WHOLE SHIFT.

PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH




JOB TITLE

B2B Marketing Consultant

JOB ID

STASAM1R

INDUSTRY

Product Design and B2B Design Marketing

LOCATION

LATAM Only

JOB STATUS

Full Time

WORK SCHEDULE

8:30am to 5pm Monday to Friday EST (Flexible)

SALARY

1400 to 1800/month

TARGET START DATE

ASAP




Role Overview

The client is seeking a B2B Marketing Consultant to support the next stage of growth for its hospitality design licensing business.

This role is ideal for a strategic yet hands on marketer who can help connect brand storytelling, partner growth, sales rep enablement, customer insights, content execution, and marketing operations into one cohesive growth engine.

The consultant will work closely with leadership and the internal team to strengthen partner relationships, support licensed product collections, improve visibility into sales and specification opportunities, and build systems that help the business scale beyond founder led marketing.

This is not a traditional social media only or content only role. The right person should be able to move comfortably between strategy, execution, relationship management, project coordination, and commercial thinking.




About the Client

The client is a New York based design studio rooted in hospitality, known for creating trend forward licensed product collections across categories including flooring, wallcovering, textiles, tile, leather, furniture, and more.

The company partners with leading manufacturers to bring its designs into hospitality, commercial, and interior design projects around the world. The role of marketing is to strengthen the brand, support partner growth, increase product visibility, and create more opportunities for licensed collections to be specified in real projects.




Key Responsibilities

1. Partner Growth & Sales Enablement

• Support partner growth initiatives across manufacturers and licensed product categories.

• Help organize and maintain partner contact lists, sales rep directories, territories, and key relationship data.

• Coordinate with partner teams to improve visibility into sample activity, quote activity, project opportunities, and sales rep needs.

• Create or update sales enablement materials that help reps and partners present products more effectively.

• Support partner presentations, lunch and learns, CEUs, showroom activations, events, and co op marketing opportunities.

• Identify cross category opportunities where multiple product lines may be relevant to the same project.

2. Rep Ambassador Network

• Help launch, manage, and evolve the Rep Ambassador Network.

• Support the selection of pilot markets, partner approved reps, and ambassador candidates.

• Help coordinate workshops, rep feedback sessions, follow ups, and ongoing touchpoints.

• Maintain a simple tracking system for rep engagement, project intelligence, handoffs, sample/spec activity, and partner feedback.

• Support the development of ambassador tools, recognition ideas, badges, merchandise, and sales resources.

• Help turn field insights from reps into actionable marketing and partner growth opportunities.

3. Voice of Customer & Market Intelligence

• Conduct interviews with sales reps, interior designers, procurement contacts, and partner stakeholders.

• Capture insights around product feedback, specification behavior, market differences, sales objections, lost opportunities, and customer needs.

• Summarize findings into clear insights, recommendations, and action items.

• Help identify patterns by geography, product category, partner, designer segment, and project type.

• Use insights to improve content, product storytelling, sales tools, and partner support.

4. Marketing Strategy & 90 Day Planning

• Support the development of short term and long term B2B marketing priorities.

• Help define what initiatives should be prioritized, paused, simplified, or scaled.

• Create actionable plans that connect marketing activity to partner growth, sales enablement, brand awareness, and specification opportunities.

• Help the team stay focused on the most important growth levers rather than trying to execute too many disconnected initiatives at once.

5. Content, Social & Thought Leadership

• Support content planning and execution across LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook, blog/editorial, newsletters, and partner channels.

• Help translate product collections, trends, and design concepts into clear, engaging, audience relevant storytelling.

• Draft captions, social posts, article summaries, email copy, partner updates, and thought leadership content.

• Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders to gather assets, organize content calendars, and support publishing workflows.

• Maintain a consistent, design forward, sophisticated, strategic, warm, and commercially relevant brand voice.

6. Lead Capture, CRM & Marketing Operations

• Support improvements to website lead capture, contact forms, inquiry routing, and follow up processes.

• Help organize and maintain contact databases, lead lists, partner lists, rep directories, and outreach trackers.

• Support light CRM or spreadsheet based tracking for leads, samples, quotes, reps, designers, and partner activity.

• Help create simple reporting structures that make it easier to understand what is working and where opportunities may be getting lost.

• Ensure marketing and sales related information is documented clearly so the team can act on it.

7. Events, Product Launches & Partner Activations

• Support planning and follow up for trade shows, partner events, showroom presentations, market visits, product launches, and hospitality design activations.

• Help prepare outreach emails, invite lists, event talking points, content capture plans, and post event follow up.

• Coordinate with partners to identify opportunities for collections to be featured in presentations, events, CEUs, lunch and learns, and client meetings.




Ideal Candidate Profile

The ideal candidate is a strong communicator, strategic thinker, and organized executor who understands how to connect marketing activity to business growth.

They should be comfortable working in a small entrepreneurial environment where priorities can shift quickly and where the ability to build structure is just as important as the ability to execute.




Required Strengths

• Strong B2B marketing experience

• Excellent writing and communication skills

• Experience supporting partner marketing, sales enablement, or business development initiatives

• Ability to manage multiple projects, timelines, stakeholders, and follow ups

• Strong organizational skills and comfort building trackers, lists, calendars, and simple operating systems

• Ability to turn conversations and insights into clear summaries, recommendations, and next steps

• Comfort working directly with founders, senior leaders, partners, sales reps, and creative teams

• Strong sense of brand voice, visual storytelling, and premium positioning

• Ability to work independently and proactively

Preferred Experience

• Experience in hospitality design, interiors, architecture, commercial design, lifestyle, luxury, or design adjacent industries

• Experience working with licensed product collections or manufacturer partners

• Familiarity with sales rep ecosystems, specification processes, design firms, procurement, or commercial interiors

• Experience with CRM, HubSpot, spreadsheets, contact management, or marketing operations

• Experience with Canva, social media scheduling, LinkedIn content, newsletters, or content calendars

• Experience conducting customer interviews or Voice of Customer research

Tools & Platforms

Experience with some of the following is helpful:

• Canva

• LinkedIn, Instagram, Facebook

• HubSpot or similar CRM tools

• Google Sheets / Excel

• Google Drive / Dropbox / shared file systems

• Basecamp or project management tools

• Email marketing platforms

• Website CMS or form tools

• AI tools for writing, content support, research, and workflow efficiency

Success in This Role Looks Like

Within the first 30 to 60 days, the consultant should be able to:

• Understand the business model, partners, product categories, and core growth opportunities.

• Organize key partner, rep, designer, and project information into usable systems.

• Support the Rep Ambassador Network launch plan and workshop preparation.

• Improve visibility into sales rep activity, partner needs, and lead follow up.

• Create clear, polished content and partner communications.

• Help leadership prioritize the highest impact marketing and growth initiatives.

Within 90 days, success would include:

• A stronger partner and rep database.

• A clearer process for capturing and following up on leads, samples, quotes, and project signals.

• A launched or launch ready Rep Ambassador Network pilot.

• Consistent content and storytelling support.

• Stronger alignment between marketing, partner support, and sales enablement.

• Clear recommendations for what to scale next.

description:
About the job

minu es la plataforma líder en compensación y beneficios para empleados. Queremos evolucionar la forma en que compensamos a los colaboradores con una plataforma digital que ofrece +50 beneficios, en tres pilares: salud financiera, salud física y salud mental. 




Es reconocida como una de las Top 10 Startups en México por Linkedin, una de las 30 promesas de negocios por Forbes México, fue finalista del Global Tech Innovator de KPMG y ganadora de las 100 más PRO de Pro Network. Cuenta con el respaldo de los mejores fondos de inversión en el mundo: Village Global (Bill Gates, Jeff Bezos, Mark Zuckerberg), QED Investors, Fintech Collective, Mountain Nazca, Next Billion Ventures y Flourish Ventures. 




Valoramos la construcción de un equipo diverso y te invitamos a enviar tu solicitud independientemente de tu origen étnico, racial o socioeconómico, expresión e identidad de género, orientación sexual, edad o diversidad funcional . minu es un "Mejor Lugar para Trabajar LGBT" según HRC Equidad MX.




Resumen del puesto




minu busca continuar desarrollando su canal de alianzas para incrementar su cartera de clientes mediante la generación de oportunidades de venta para el equipo comercial. Como Analista de Partnerships, apoyarás en la ejecución y seguimiento del programa de alianzas, fortaleciendo la relación con aliados actuales e identificando nuevas oportunidades de colaboración. Este rol será clave para coordinar iniciativas con partners, dar seguimiento a oportunidades generadas por el canal y analizar el desempeño de las alianzas.




Las alianzas de minu incluyen instituciones financieras, softwares de nómina, distribuidores, embajadores de marca, asociaciones y cámaras empresariales. Este canal ha sido históricamente uno de los más efectivos para la adquisición de nuevos clientes. Esta posición reporta al Director de Growth.




Lo que harás




Apoyar en la ejecución del programa de alianzas de minu.
Identificar y prospectar posibles aliados estratégicos.
Dar seguimiento a oportunidades de venta generadas a través del canal de partners.
Apoyar en el proceso de negociación y formalización de nuevas alianzas.
Coordinar con sales enablement el pago de las comisiones de los aliados.
Coordinar con marketing la realización de eventos con aliados.

Licenciatura en Economía, Finanzas o Ingeniería Industrial.
1 a 4 años de experiencia previa en áreas de alianzas o desarrollo de negocios.
Capacidad de relacionamiento con terceros.

title: SaaS Account Manager - México
skills: Tú quieres desarrollarte en la gestión de clientes y entorno SaaS.
Tienes una escritura excelente y unas habilidades comunicativas poderosas –¿lleva coma el vocativo?–, y te sientes muy cómodo/a trabajando en un ambiente eléctrico y de alto rendimiento..
Eres una persona con una alta inteligencia emocional y con la capacidad de entender y empatizar con las necesidades de otros.
Además, eres amigable, sonríes cuando hablas y eres tan fuerte como Arnold –gestionas el ‘no’ a las mil maravillas–.
Eres una persona curiosa con criterio para detectar mejoras.
Te manejas bien con la tecnología, esto es importante para encajar bien con nuestra cultura, ya que somos doers.
Te pondrás en contacto con concesionarios para cerrar con ellos una demo del software.
Desarrollarás relaciones sanas y estratégicas con los posibles clientes.
Harás la demo del producto, intentarás mantener la compostura cuando el cliente alucine..
Durante la prueba gratuita, tendrás que configurar la herramienta, formar a los usuarios y dar buenas prácticas.
Así que cultivarás una relación con tu cliente, comunicándole novedades y aportándole astucias y buenas prácticas a lo largo del tiempo.
Contrato a jornada completa –40 horas semanales–.
Contrato indefinido –queremos a alguien para siempre ❤–.
Trabajo en remoto –presencial en Madrid durante las primeras semanas para empaparse de la cultura de Walcu, conectar con el equipo y formarse–.
Podrás decidir tu horario de entrada y de salida –aunque siempre hay que cubrir de 10:00 a 13:00 de lunes a viernes y de 17:00 a 19:00 de lunes a jueves–. ¡El viernes por la tarde de juerga!
description:
About the job

Somos una start-up tecnológica que ofrece un CRM (herramienta de gestión comercial) en la nube (SaaS) a concesionarios. En 5 años ya somos unos 45 empleados y facturamos €5M anuales. Nos hemos convertido en líderes del mercado español y ya tenemos presencia en otros países. Bla, bla, bla…Lo importante no son los hitos alcanzados sino nuestra cultura. Todo el mundo tiene voz para contribuir a la mejora de la empresa. Somos confiantes, contamos con que haces tu trabajo haciendo esfuerzos honestos. Valoramos el impacto de tus contribuciones individuales y la influencia que puedas tener sobre el trabajo de tus compañeros. Somos un equipo audaz, transparente y nos gusta la acción.




Sobre ti:




No seremos nosotros quienes digamos cómo eres tú, salvo en este caso, que sí lo haremos. 🙂

Tú quieres desarrollarte en la gestión de clientes y entorno SaaS.

Durante la prueba gratuita, tendrás que configurar la herramienta, formar a los usuarios y dar buenas prácticas.
Así que cultivarás una relación con tu cliente, comunicándole novedades y aportándole astucias y buenas prácticas a lo largo del tiempo.

title: Insights Analyst
skills: Interpret customer requirements, translate them into clear reporting deliverables, including generating dashboards, query building and recurring or ad hoc report formats.

Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of earned and owned media data, including social listening outputs, news coverage, and content performance, to identify key narratives, opportunities, and risks.
Craft contextual insights based on performance metrics and KPIs, turning raw data into clear, concise storylines.
Maintain visibility across reporting timelines, proactively communicate progress or blockers, and ensure methodological consistency and quality across all deliverables
Uphold visual and formatting consistency across deliverables, proofreading for clarity, data integrity, and narrative cohesion
Address customer inquiries and concerns regarding delivered reports, contributing to training and initiatives to enhance team knowledge and efficiency.
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of data to provide clients with insights on brand perception, performance, and industry trends.
Own the reporting workflow from setup to delivery, ensuring clarity in expectations, smooth coordination with internal teams, and high-quality outputs for clients
Utilize Meltwater software, Excel, and PowerPoint to extract data, synthesize findings, and package insights in a way that informs strategic decisions.
Develop strategies to improve report quality and objectives based on client feedback, ensuring timely completion of deliverables with high quality by effectively coordinating with the team.
Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Social Sciences, Communications, Marketing, Media Studies, Data Analytics, or related fields emphasizing research and analysis.
0-2 years of relevant work experience (client-facing roles are advantageous).
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English, with a particular emphasis on proficient report writing in English.
Proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint & Excel, including PivotTable, VLOOKUP, and similar functions.
Meticulous attention to detail, adept at handling recurring tasks and ensuring data integrity.
Proficient report writing skills, covering both qualitative and quantitative aspects.
Customer-focused mindset with a commitment to exceptional client servicing.
Critical thinking skills, facilitating insights derived from data analysis.
Strong interest in current affairs, mainstream media, and social media trends.
Comfortable working autonomously and collaborating effectively with remote team members, including reporting to managers situated in distant locations.
A keen interest in learning, adaptability, and resilience in a dynamic work environment.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, particularly in report writing.
Willingness to embrace the best of both worlds with our hybrid work schedule. This role requires you to be in the office 3 days a week.
The ability to legally work in the country of hire is required for this position.
Enjoy comprehensive paid time off that allows you to have an enhanced work-life balance.
Excellent medical, dental, and vision options
Complimentary CalmApp subscription for you and your loved ones, because mental wellness matters.
Energetic work environment with a hybrid work style, providing the balance you need.
Thrive within our inclusive community and seize ongoing professional development opportunities to elevate your career
description:
About the job

Description

What We’re Looking For

Join Meltwater's Client Insights team as Insights Analyst, where you'll play a key role in transforming data into meaningful narratives for our clients. You’ll contribute to bespoke reporting, using both quantitative and qualitative analysis to uncover trends, identify opportunities, and guide strategic decisions.

In this role, you’ll take ownership of report delivery workflows, coordinating internally as needed to ensure insights are delivered with consistency, clarity, and impact. Your work will blend data storytelling, critical thinking, and collaborative execution. At Meltwater, you'll immerse yourself in an environment fostering continuous growth, where mentorship and inclusive leadership are fundamental principles rather than mere catchphrases. Collaborate with experienced colleagues and respected leaders, continually evolving alongside industry trends. Within this dynamic environment, you'll thrive within a culture emphasizing empowerment and autonomy, making significant contributions to our expanding organization's success.

What You'll Do:


Interpret customer requirements, translate them into clear reporting deliverables, including generating dashboards, query building and recurring or ad hoc report formats. 
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of earned and owned media data, including social listening outputs, news coverage, and content performance, to identify key narratives, opportunities, and risks. 
Craft contextual insights based on performance metrics and KPIs, turning raw data into clear, concise storylines. 
Maintain visibility across reporting timelines, proactively communicate progress or blockers, and ensure methodological consistency and quality across all deliverables
Uphold visual and formatting consistency across deliverables, proofreading for clarity, data integrity, and narrative cohesion
Address customer inquiries and concerns regarding delivered reports, contributing to training and initiatives to enhance team knowledge and efficiency. 
Conduct quantitative and qualitative analysis of data to provide clients with insights on brand perception, performance, and industry trends. 
Own the reporting workflow from setup to delivery, ensuring clarity in expectations, smooth coordination with internal teams, and high-quality outputs for clients
Utilize Meltwater software, Excel, and PowerPoint to extract data, synthesize findings, and package insights in a way that informs strategic decisions. 
Develop strategies to improve report quality and objectives based on client feedback, ensuring timely completion of deliverables with high quality by effectively coordinating with the team. 


What You'll Bring:


Bachelor's degree in a relevant field such as Social Sciences, Communications, Marketing, Media Studies, Data Analytics, or related fields emphasizing research and analysis. 
0-2 years of relevant work experience (client-facing roles are advantageous). 
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English, with a particular emphasis on proficient report writing in English. 
Proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint & Excel, including PivotTable, VLOOKUP, and similar functions. 
Meticulous attention to detail, adept at handling recurring tasks and ensuring data integrity. 
Proficient report writing skills, covering both qualitative and quantitative aspects. 
Customer-focused mindset with a commitment to exceptional client servicing. 
Critical thinking skills, facilitating insights derived from data analysis. 
Strong interest in current affairs, mainstream media, and social media trends. 
Comfortable working autonomously and collaborating effectively with remote team members, including reporting to managers situated in distant locations. 
A keen interest in learning, adaptability, and resilience in a dynamic work environment. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills in English, particularly in report writing. 
Willingness to embrace the best of both worlds with our hybrid work schedule. This role requires you to be in the office 3 days a week. 
The ability to legally work in the country of hire is required for this position. 


What We Offer:


Enjoy comprehensive paid time off that allows you to have an enhanced work-life balance. 
Excellent medical, dental, and vision options
Complimentary CalmApp subscription for you and your loved ones, because mental wellness matters. 
Energetic work environment with a hybrid work style, providing the balance you need. 
Thrive within our inclusive community and seize ongoing professional development opportunities to elevate your career


Where You’ll Work: Lago Alberto 375, Suite 200 Granada, Miguel Hidalgo 11320 Ciudad de México, CDMX, Mexico

Our Story

At Meltwater, we believe that when you have the right people in the right environment, great things happen.

Our best-in-class technology empowers our 27,000 customers around the world to make better business decisions through data. But we can’t do that without our global team of developers, innovators, problem-solvers, and high-performers who embrace challenges and find new solutions for our customers.

Our award-winning global culture drives everything we do and creates an environment where our employees can make an impact, learn every day, feel a sense of belonging, and celebrate each other’s successes along the way.

We are innovators at the core who see the potential in people, ideas and technologies. Together, we challenge ourselves to go big, be bold, and build best-in-class solutions for our customers.

We’re proud of our diverse team of 2,200+ employees in 50 locations across 25 countries around the world. No matter where you are, you’ll work with people who care about your success and get the support you need to unlock new heights in your career.

We are Meltwater. Inspired by innovation, powered by people.

Equal Employment Opportunity Statement

Meltwater is an Equal Opportunity Employer and Prohibits Discrimination and Harassment of Any Kind: At Meltwater, we are dedicated to fostering an inclusive and diverse workplace where every employee feels valued, respected, and empowered. We are committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity and strive to provide a work environment that is free from discrimination and harassment.

All employment decisions at Meltwater are made based on business needs, job requirements, and individual qualifications, without regard to race, color, religion or belief, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, marital status, veteran status, or any other status protected by the applicable laws and regulations.

Meltwater does not tolerate discrimination or harassment of any kind, and we actively promote a culture of respect, fairness, and inclusivity. We encourage applicants of all backgrounds, experiences, and abilities to apply and join us in our mission to drive innovation and make a positive impact in the world.

We may use AI to assist in aspects of our hiring process, such as reviewing candidate information, capturing interview notes, and supporting interview activities. These tools are used only to assist our team through increased efficiencies. Every output continues to be reviewed by humans, and all hiring decisions are made by humans. About the job

Descripción del puesto En el puesto de Especialista en CRM y automatización en Centro SanaMente te encargarás de diseñar, configurar y optimizar flujos de trabajo automatizados para la gestión de personas usuarias y pacientes potenciales. En tu día a día segmentarás bases de datos, crearás campañas automatizadas (emails, recordatorios, seguimientos) y monitorizarás su rendimiento para proponer mejoras continuas. Colaborarás con los equipos de marketing y atención al paciente para definir procesos, mejorar la experiencia de la persona usuaria y aumentar la conversión y fidelización. También documentarás los procesos, elaborarás informes periódicos y ayudarás a implementar nuevas herramientas o integraciones relacionadas con el CRM. Este es un puesto de media jornada (part time) y se desarrolla en modalidad remota.

title: Senior Market Manager
skills: Lead net-new host and listing acquisition in assigned markets, moving qualified leads through the funnel to meet and exceed quarterly supply goals
Prospect and identify high-quality homes through external research, networking, and partnerships with luxury real estate professionals, property managers, and operators
Qualify incoming leads and deliver fully vetted hosts and listings to the onboarding team with clear handoff documentation
Compellingly articulate Airbnb Luxe's value proposition across all formats — phone, email, in-person — tailored to the luxury audience
Own new market launches end-to-end, including lead generation, host recruitment, coordination with Home Quality and Guest Services, and post-launch performance monitoring
Develop a nuanced understanding of your markets: desirable neighborhoods, architectural styles, design aesthetics, pricing trends, seasonality, and the competitive landscape
Build and maintain localized pitches that resonate with luxury homeowners and real estate professionals in your region
Manage a portfolio of Luxe hosts, serving as their primary point of contact and trusted advisor
Conduct regular business reviews and optimization calls to uncover challenges, identify growth opportunities, and provide data-driven recommendations
Support onboarding of new listings for assigned hosts, ensuring a seamless experience
Design and deploy strategies to manage and optimize long-tail hosts across your markets
Improve listing bookability through performance analysis and targeted host education
Act as the primary liaison between assigned hosts and internal teams (Guest Services, Content & Quality, Product, Policy) for escalated issues and strategic initiatives
Serve as an effective "air traffic controller" — routing host requests to the right internal resources while maintaining direct host accessibility
Cascade host feedback to relevant stakeholders to inform product and policy decisions
Educate hosts on significant product updates, policy changes, and platform best practices
8+ years of relevant experience in sales, account management, business development, or strategic partnerships — ideally within luxury travel, hospitality, or real estate
Demonstrated track record of hitting or exceeding acquisition and/or revenue targets in a fast-paced, metrics-driven environment
Experience working cross-functionally across teams to influence change and drive results
Familiarity with CRM tools and the ability to maintain rigorous pipeline hygiene and reporting
Strong executive presence with the ability to build credibility and influence senior decision-makers both internally and within the luxury industry
Excellent negotiation skills and the ability to close complex, high-value partnerships
Sharp market instincts — you can quickly assess a neighborhood, identify a trend, and translate that into actionable strategy
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; able to craft compelling pitches and maintain polished host-facing correspondence
Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and stakeholder relationships simultaneously
Comfortable operating with ambiguity and adapting quickly in an ever-evolving environment
English and Spanish language proficiency required.
Deep alignment with Airbnb's mission and a passion for the luxury travel and homes space
A consultative, relationship-first approach — you build trust before you close deals
Proactive, solutions-oriented, and self-directed; you don't wait for problems to escalate
Collaborative by nature — you understand that success in this role is a team sport
description:
About the job

Airbnb was born in 2007 when two hosts welcomed three guests to their San Francisco home, and has since grown to over 5 million hosts who have welcomed over 2 billion guest arrivals in almost every country across the globe. Every day, hosts offer unique stays and experiences that make it possible for guests to connect with communities in a more authentic way.

The Difference You Will Make

As a Luxe Senior Market Manager, you will drive supply growth for Airbnb Luxe in your assigned geographies — acquiring new listings and cultivating host relationships that define Luxe. You will serve as the on-the-ground market expert, combining deep knowledge of luxury real estate and travel landscape with a consultative, relationship-first approach to acquisition and account management.

You will work with Luxe Guest Services, Content & Quality, and cross-functional teams to ensure every home meets Airbnb Luxe's standards. This role is equally focused on strategic growth and day-to-day excellence - you'll be equally comfortable prospecting net-new hosts, conducting business reviews, and rolling up your sleeves to solve host challenges.

A Typical Day

Supply Acquisition & Pipeline Development


Lead net-new host and listing acquisition in assigned markets, moving qualified leads through the funnel to meet and exceed quarterly supply goals
Prospect and identify high-quality homes through external research, networking, and partnerships with luxury real estate professionals, property managers, and operators
Qualify incoming leads and deliver fully vetted hosts and listings to the onboarding team with clear handoff documentation
Compellingly articulate Airbnb Luxe's value proposition across all formats — phone, email, in-person — tailored to the luxury audience


Market Development & Launches


Own new market launches end-to-end, including lead generation, host recruitment, coordination with Home Quality and Guest Services, and post-launch performance monitoring
Develop a nuanced understanding of your markets: desirable neighborhoods, architectural styles, design aesthetics, pricing trends, seasonality, and the competitive landscape
Build and maintain localized pitches that resonate with luxury homeowners and real estate professionals in your region


Host Account Management & Optimization


Manage a portfolio of Luxe hosts, serving as their primary point of contact and trusted advisor
Conduct regular business reviews and optimization calls to uncover challenges, identify growth opportunities, and provide data-driven recommendations
Support onboarding of new listings for assigned hosts, ensuring a seamless experience
Design and deploy strategies to manage and optimize long-tail hosts across your markets
Improve listing bookability through performance analysis and targeted host education


Cross-Functional Collaboration & Escalations


Act as the primary liaison between assigned hosts and internal teams (Guest Services, Content & Quality, Product, Policy) for escalated issues and strategic initiatives
Serve as an effective "air traffic controller" — routing host requests to the right internal resources while maintaining direct host accessibility
Cascade host feedback to relevant stakeholders to inform product and policy decisions
Educate hosts on significant product updates, policy changes, and platform best practices


Expertise


8+ years of relevant experience in sales, account management, business development, or strategic partnerships — ideally within luxury travel, hospitality, or real estate
Demonstrated track record of hitting or exceeding acquisition and/or revenue targets in a fast-paced, metrics-driven environment
Experience working cross-functionally across teams to influence change and drive results
Familiarity with CRM tools and the ability to maintain rigorous pipeline hygiene and reporting


Skills & Competencies


Strong executive presence with the ability to build credibility and influence senior decision-makers both internally and within the luxury industry
Excellent negotiation skills and the ability to close complex, high-value partnerships
Sharp market instincts — you can quickly assess a neighborhood, identify a trend, and translate that into actionable strategy
Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; able to craft compelling pitches and maintain polished host-facing correspondence
Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple priorities, projects, and stakeholder relationships simultaneously
Comfortable operating with ambiguity and adapting quickly in an ever-evolving environment
English and Spanish language proficiency required.


Mindset


Deep alignment with Airbnb's mission and a passion for the luxury travel and homes space
A consultative, relationship-first approach — you build trust before you close deals
Proactive, solutions-oriented, and self-directed; you don't wait for problems to escalate
Collaborative by nature — you understand that success in this role is a team sport


Location:

This role is based out of Mexico City, Mexico overseeing LATAM. It requires up to 50% travel to markets across LATAM, as well as occasional team off-sites.

Our Commitment To Inclusion & Belonging

Airbnb is committed to working with the broadest talent pool possible. We believe diverse ideas foster innovation and engagement, and allow us to attract creatively-led people, and to develop the best products, services and solutions. All qualified individuals are encouraged to apply.

How We'll Take Care Of You

Our job titles may span more than one career level. The actual base pay is dependent upon many factors, such as: training, transferable skills, work experience, business needs and market demands. The base pay range shown below is monthly, is subject to change and may be modified in the future. This role may also be eligible for bonus, equity, benefits, and Employee Travel Credits.

Mexico Monthly Pay Range

$100,000—$125,000 MXN

title: Desarrollador de Chatbot
description:
About the job

Allianz Partners

Is a world leader in B2B2C insurance and assistance, offering global solutions that span international health and life, travel insurance, automotive and assistance. Customer driven, our innovative experts are redefining insurance services by delivering future-ready, high-tech high-touch products and solutions that go beyond traditional insurance.

Our products are embedded seamlessly into our partners’ businesses or sold directly to customers and are available through four commercial brands: Allianz Assistance, Allianz Automotive, Allianz Travel and Allianz Care. Present in 75 countries, our 21,100 employees speak 70 languages, handle over 71 million cases each year, and are motivated to go the extra mile to offer peace of mind to our customers around the world.

We're looking for our new members!




This role is acting as global

AzP Conversational AI (CAI) Solution Engineer, located in the GCC Mexico.

Its mission is to help to develop and deploy effective Chatbots; Voicebots that meet the specific local needs.

This involves working closely with various internal and external stakeholders to understand the requirements, designing and building conversational AI systems that integrate with their existing technologies and core systems (e.g. CRM) and testing and optimizing the solution to ensure they deliver a high-quality user experience.




In order to be successful in this role, one will need:




Required Experience:




Knowledge of Chatbots, Conversational AI frameworks and technologies, such as STT (Speech-To-Text), TTS (Text-To-Speech), SSML (Speech Synthesis Markup Language) and NLP (Natural Language Processing), LLMs (Large Language Models).




Experience with Java Script, APIs, and other relevant technologies.




Understanding of the

principles of Conversational design and user experience (UX) for Conversational interfaces.




Expertise in programming languages such as JavaScript, Python, or similar

programming languages.




Understanding of

understanding of REST APIs and web services.




Knowledge of Agile methodology and experience in working with project management tools.




Experience in large-scale enterprise infrastructure and application landscape.




Advanced English level and Computer Science/Systems Engineering degree

is required.




Conversational AI Platform certification (Infobip, Cognigy etc.) is a plus.




Key Responsibilities:







Designing and developing chatbots, conversational flows, user interfaces and integration points/ extensions for Chat/Voicebots.




Developing and documenting processes and procedures for building and deploying Conversational AI solution.




Support Conversational AI operations projects with expertise.




Documenting technical changes, workflow related tasks and provide development status update where applicable.




Collaborating with other engineers and stakeholders to ensure that solutions are designed to be scalable, reliable, and maintainable.




Troubleshoot and resolve issues.




Continuously and self-independently drives asset enhancements and concept creation.




Providing technical guidance and support to AzP stakeholders during the development and deployment of their Conversational AI use cases.




Offer:




Direct hiring.




Remote work (must be based in Mexico City).




Full time

Monday - Friday schedule.




35-40k MXN Monthly salary (according to experience) + Food Coupons

+ Law benefits + Annual bonus + Health Insurance + Major Medical Expenses Insurance + Saving Fund.







Allianz Group is one of the most trusted insurance and asset management companies in the world. Caring for our employees, their ambitions, dreams and challenges, is what makes us a unique employer. Together we can build an environment where everyone feels empowered and has the confidence to explore, to grow and to shape a better future for our customers and the world around us.




We at Allianz believe in a diverse and inclusive workforce and are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. We encourage you to bring your whole self to work, no matter where you are from, what you look like, who you love or what you believe in. We therefore welcome applications regardless of ethnicity or cultural background, age, gender, nationality, religion, disability or sexual orientation. Great to have you on board. Let's care for tomorrow.

description:
About the job

About The Role

As a Strategic Finance Sr. Associate for Delivery in LatAm, you'll be a key member of the Strategic Finance team with regional scope. This role offers unique visibility across the organization and the opportunity to influence strategic decision-making through data-driven financial analysis and insights.

You'll partner closely with Central Operations, Strategy & Planning, and HQ Finance to advise stakeholders, support financial planning, and contribute to high-impact projects that drive growth and efficiency. If you're passionate about solving complex problems, love working cross-functionally, and want to help shape the future of Uber in Latin America, this is the role for you.

What The Candidate Will Do


 Financial planning and analysis: Act as the finance point of contact for your country, supporting monthly, quarterly, annual, and long-range planning processes 
 Business partnership: Provide financial insights to business leaders and help them optimize capital allocation and drive efficiency 
 Reporting and variance analysis: Deliver monthly performance reports and identify risks, opportunities, and mitigation strategies 
 Collaboration with accounting and central teams: Align financials with business strategy and improve understanding of actuals 
 Strategic insights: Apply business judgment to support decision-making across pricing, promotions, and marketplace dynamics 
 Regional reporting: Analyze results across all countries under your responsibility and provide insights on regional performance drivers 
 Operational improvement: Help scale the function by implementing new tools and processes that enable fast and accurate decision-making 
 Ad hoc analysis: Run deep-dives to identify cost-saving opportunities, growth levers, and risk mitigation strategies 
 Act as a change agent, influencing teams across the organization with data and strategic thinking 


Basic Qualifications


 Bachelor's degree in Business, Economics, Finance, Engineering, or a related quantitative field 
 From 6 to 8 years of experience in Corporate Finance, FP&A, Corporate Development, Investment Banking, and/or Consulting 
 Advanced financial modeling skills with efficient, scalable, and intuitive models 


Preferred Qualifications


 Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to influence across functions and levels 
 Strong stakeholder management: ability to lead across teams and challenge constructively when needed 
 Self-starter with a proactive mindset, strong goal orientation, and ownership mentality 
 Ability to balance strategic thinking with attention to operational detail 
 Outstanding presentation skills 
 Attention to detail with the ability to execute quickly and accurately in a dynamic environment

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

title: Volunteer Marketing and Communications Lead to Connect Stakeholders Working to End Deep Polarization with Colombia Construye Confianza
description:
About the job

This is an opportunity to work with an Ashoka Fellow 

Ashoka Fellows are leading social entrepreneurs that have been taken through a rigorous selection process, testing the quality of their ideas and character, to become part of the Ashoka Fellowship and Network. Ashoka Fellows take on the challenges of building innovative system change ideas to make and spread social impact in a world wrought with challenges. They refuse to let the ‘impossible’ stand in their way to creating a better world.

This is an unpaid, volunteer opportunity

About The Affiliated Organization

Colombia Construye Confianza (CCC) was founded to address one of Colombia's most urgent social challenges: deep polarization among citizens who have lost the ability to trust one another. We work with communities, organizations, and institutions to transform the way people relate, building the social trust that democracy and coexistence require. Our flagship methodology, BAKONDO, has reached over 3,200 people over more than 20 years, earning recognition from the World Economic Forum, Coca-Cola, and One Young World. Through experiential education, play, and art, we bring together volunteers and vulnerable communities to form empathetic leaders. Our most powerful program, BAKONDO Paz, unites ex-combatants, victims of the armed conflict, and social leaders to run a camp for children in vulnerable situations, turning former adversaries into builders of peace.

For more information, please visit www.colombiaconstruyeconfianza.org

Job Description

Colombia Construye Confianza is seeking a strategic marketing volunteer to help us design and deploy a comprehensive communications strategy that connects us with all our key stakeholders. We recently developed our brand concept and initial materials with a creative agency, and now need someone to take that foundation and turn it into an active, results-driven marketing operation. The volunteer will design the overall communications and marketing strategy for CCC and its initiatives, including our BAKONDO bootcamps. They will define the right channels, messages, and tactics to attract volunteers, corporate partners, donors, universities, and impact investors. They will also help manage and optimize our social media presence across Instagram, Facebook, LinkedIn, and WhatsApp, and support the team in executing campaigns consistently. Their work will directly determine how many people discover, trust, and join our mission, making it essential to the growth and sustainability of the organization. Please let us know today if you can help!

Volunteer Logistics

Estimated hours required per week: 5

The estimated duration of the project is: 3-6 months

This position is Virtual (Remote)

Language Requirements

Spanish

Desired Skills And Experience

We are looking for a professional with demonstrated experience in marketing strategy and digital communications, ideally within the social impact, NGO, or purpose-driven brand space. The ideal candidate has strong skills in content strategy, social media management, and audience segmentation, and knows how to translate a compelling mission into messages that move people to act. Experience working with multiple stakeholder audiences (corporate, institutional, and grassroots) is highly valued. Proficiency in Spanish is required; English is a plus. A background in impact communications, social entrepreneurship, or international development will help the volunteer hit the ground running.

En DICKA Logistics nos encontramos en búsqueda de talento para integrarse a nuestro equipo comercial como Business Developer Executive.




Buscamos una persona con experiencia en el sector logístico, preferentemente en empresas 3PL, transporte, distribución, freight forwarding o soluciones integrales de cadena de suministro.




La posición requiere conocimiento del proceso comercial end to end, desde la prospección de nuevos clientes hasta el cierre de oportunidades, asegurando una adecuada identificación de necesidades, construcción de propuestas, análisis de viabilidad operativa y seguimiento puntual de cada oportunidad comercial.




Principales responsabilidades:
Prospectar y desarrollar nuevas oportunidades de negocio en el sector logístico.
Identificar necesidades de clientes en servicios 3PL, transporte, distribución, almacenaje o soluciones logísticas integrales.

title: Partnerships Associate
description:
About the job

Hiring: Partnerships Associate for Million Dollar Sellers, a top-tier ecommerce community.




You’ll own the full partner lifecycle, close new partners, then make sure every commitment runs cleanly.


ABOUT MDS

An exclusive community of 700+ seven- and eight-figure Amazon and ecommerce founders. $12B+ in combined member sales. Nearly 100 events per year. Backed by industry-leading partners.


WHAT YOU’LL DO

— Identify and close new partners across affiliates, event sponsorships, and community offerings, owning outreach from first contact to signed agreement

— Onboard every new partner completely into the database, app listing, newsletter, offers, and deliverable tracker before anyone has to ask

— Track and execute all partner deliverables across events, campaigns, and affiliate programs so nothing promised slips

— Send the weekly Partners Newsletter every Tuesday and maintain the partner database, contracts, offers, and KPI tracking

— Produce AI-powered Community Pulse summaries surfacing partner mentions and insights from across community channels


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

— Experience in partnerships, sales, or BD, with a track record of closing deals, not just managing them

— Equally comfortable sending a cold outreach email and cleaning up a database

— Operational discipline: you onboard fully, track deliverables proactively, and treat the database as a live system, not an archive

— Familiarity with CRMs and ops tools, Airtable, ClickUp, SignNow, or similar

— Comfort using AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT) as part of your regular workflow

— Strong written English, you’ll be writing outreach, a weekly newsletter, and partner comms

— Based in LATAM (Mexico, Argentina preferred). Travel required minimum 3x per year

— Available 10AM–3PM EST core hours




COMPENSATION

— USD $2,000–$3,000/month (band reflects balance of sales track record and operational depth)

— Tiered bonus plan tied to annual revenue goals

— Long-term, multi-year engagement

— Direct line to the VP of Revenue




HOW TO APPLY

Apply through our form: https://forms.clickup.com/90161589637/f/2kz0tbc5-516/HN6BGAJ1CDZMH3QDD7




You’ll need: CV, a Loom intro, and 5 minutes.

We respond to every applicant within 48 hours.
