About the job

Agentic AI –Consultant – Americas Delivery Mexico (ADMX) 




Are you an experienced, passionate pioneer in technology? An Agentic AI/Conversational Designer who wants to work in a collaborative environment? As an experienced Agentic AI/Conversational Designer, you will have the ability to share new ideas and collaborate on projects as a consultant without the extensive demands of travel. Americas Delivery Mexico (ADMX) leverages scale and talent to provide high quality, cost-effective service to our clients.




ADMX is a member of the Global Delivery Network which has presence across the world with Delivery centers in the United States, Romania, India, Spain, China, and the Philippines. ADMX is in Queretaro, Mexico. We provide consulting services to help our clients achieve a higher level of service in operational efficiency and business value. We are a team of professionals passionate about serving clients with distinction and learning, and we are driven by our purpose: Making an impact that matters for our clients, our people, and society.







Work you’ll do/Responsibilities 




As a Consultant at Deloitte, you will work within an engagement team and be responsible for supporting the overall project goals and objectives. In this role, you will interact with stakeholders and cross-functional teams. It is expected that you will be able to perform independent tasks as well as provide technical guidance to team members, as needed. 




Gather requirements, design flows, and write content for IVR, VUI/voice user interfaces, Virtual Assistants, Chatbots, and omnichannel conversational interfaces leveraging NLU and LLMs to understand users
Drive and own persona definition, functional design, process design (including scenario design, flow mapping), prototyping, testing, model/intent training, and definition of support procedures
Work with engagement leadership, project managers, conversational and generative AI architects, developers, UX designers, product managers, and consultants to strategize, design, and deliver enterprise AI servicing solutions for voice and chat
Collaborate with clients, partner professional services teams, and project leadership to identify business requirements and design voice and digital self-service solutions for diverse use cases across industries and modalities, including phone, chat, kiosks, drive-thru, and digital humans (avatars)







The Team 




Our Deloitte Customer team empowers organizations to build deeper relationships with customers through innovative strategies, advanced analytics, Generative AI, transformative technologies, and creative design. We can enhance customer experiences and drive sustained growth and customer value creation and capture, through customer and commercial strategies, digital products and innovation, marketing, commerce, sales, and service. We are a team of strategists, data scientists, operators, creatives, designers, engineers, and architects. Our team balances business strategy, technology, creativity, and ongoing managed services to solve the biggest problems that affect customers, partners, constituents, and the workforce.

Qualifications

Required

3-5+ years of consulting and/or industry experience.
Completion of course of study/Egresado in Computer Science/IT/Computer Engineering, or any pertinent field or industry, or equivalent years of experience.
Responsible for supporting and independently completing project tasks.
Advanced English level.
Relevant work experience in VUI or conversational design in or around contact centers and/or enterprise virtual assistants
Experience collaborating with conversational AI and contact center developers, architects, and other relevant team members
Group facilitation skills, including leading requirements gathering sessions, design reviews, and workshops for a variety of stakeholders from business and technology groups
Experience with requirements gathering, documentation, and tracking
Experience designing and delivering conversational and/or generative AI MVPs, pilots, and POCs
Experience with VUI / IVR flows for enterprise contact centers (using DTMF, guided speech, NLU, and/or LLMs)
Voice and/or text-based conversational flows for virtual agents or virtual assistants
Experience with flow design and prototyping tools (VoiceFlow, Figma, Sketch, Lucid Visio, etc.)
Test and Debug support deployment in Parloa or Similars (Amazon Connect / LEx -Lambda- Bedrock / GCP - dialog flow /conversation agents / playbook)
Production Level Developer, Java / Groovy
REST APIs / JSON / Javascript




Applicants must be legally authorized to work in Mexico at the time of hire; limited sponsorship may be available for this role based on business need and applicable law.




Preferred

Experience on Contact Center, VUI/IVR, Chatbot/Virtual Assistant and/or conversational AI projects
Design thinking and UX design methodology as it applies to conversational experiences and interfaces (voice/chat/multimodal)
Enterprise UX writing, content design, or content strategy experience
Experience in Financial Services & Insurance, Healthcare, Technology, Media, Telecommunications, and/or Consumer.










Our people and culture

Our inclusive culture empowers our people to be who they are, contribute to their unique perspectives, and make a difference individually and collectively. It enables us to leverage different ideas and perspectives and bring more creativity and innovation to help solve our clients' most complex challenges. This makes Deloitte one of the most rewarding places to work.




Professional development

From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe there’s always room to learn. We offer opportunities to build new skills, take on leadership opportunities and connect and grow through mentorship. From on-the-job learning experiences to formal development programs, our professionals have a variety of opportunities to continue to grow throughout their career.




Accommodations

We are committed to providing equal opportunity and reasonable accommodation for people with disabilities. To request a reasonable accommodation, contact our Talent Relations team at USTalentCICInbox@deloitte.com




As used in this posting, "Deloitte" means Deloitte Consulting LLP, a subsidiary of Deloitte LLP. Please see www.deloitte.com/us/about for a detailed description of the legal structure of Deloitte LLP and its subsidiaries.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability or protected veteran status, or any other legally protected basis, in accordance with applicable law.

title: Community Manager
description:
About the job

Job Title: Community Manager




Job Type: Full-time




Location: Remote - Latin America




Job Summary:




Join our team as a dynamic Community Manager and play a pivotal role in creating a positive, collaborative, and engaged online environment. You will be at the forefront of customer support and community moderation, proactively resolving issues, answering questions, and ensuring a welcoming experience for all members. If you thrive in fast-paced remote settings and are eager to make a meaningful impact every day, this role is for you.




Key Responsibilities:




• Monitor and moderate online communities to ensure a safe, respectful, and inclusive atmosphere.

• Address customer complaints and questions promptly with empathy and professionalism.

• De-escalate conflicts and diffuse tense situations to maintain group harmony.

• Develop proactive engagement strategies to foster a vibrant and active community.

• Collaborate with internal teams to relay feedback and insights from the community.

• Manage time efficiently to handle multiple conversations and tasks in a remote environment.

• Create clear, friendly, and effective written and verbal communications across channels.




Required Skills and Qualifications:




• Proven experience in customer support and online community moderation.

• Exceptional verbal and written communication skills; a genuine care for clear and empathetic messaging.

• Strong conflict management and de-escalation abilities.

• Self-starter attitude with high energy and a proactive approach to problem-solving.

• Excellent time management skills in a remote work setting.

• Keen attention to detail and ability to multitask under pressure.

• Adaptable, with a willingness to go the extra mile to support community needs.




Preferred Qualifications:




• Background in managing large-scale online communities.

• Experience with various community platforms and CRM tools.

• Passion for building lasting relationships and driving positive user experiences.

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

description:

SUMMARY

This role will focus on designing digital and video assets that help our global teams tell a cohesive Kids2 enterprise story - elevating our brands, capabilities, and partnerships with retailers and other key stakeholders. The ideal candidate combines strong digital design skills and storytelling. They can translate complex ideas into clear, engaging visuals and are comfortable working across presentations, social/digital content, and short-form video.

This highly cross-functional role partners closely with Category, Social, Marketing, Communications, Sales, E-commerce, and Creative teams to ensure storytelling reflects a shared direction.

title: Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d)
skills: Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.
Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.
Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.
description:
About the job

At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d) to join our North America commercial team. This is not a purely analytical role — we need someone who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to design and implement solutions, build bridges across stakeholders, and take ownership of results. You will interact directly with senior leadership, including the CEO for North America, and shape the commercial strategy of one of the world's fastest-growing mobility networks.

About The Role


Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.


About You


Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

Diseñar, implementar y optimizar campañas de CRM multicanal (email, push, WhatsApp y SMS).
Coordinar experimentos de growth y pruebas de nuevas palancas de crecimiento.
Asegurar la alineación de todas las iniciativas con los OKRs del negocio.
Trabajar de forma cross-functional con equipos de Marketing, Data, Producto, Tecnología y Atención al Cliente.

Analizar métricas clave, detectar oportunidades y optimizar continuamente el performance. - Gestionar proyectos asegurando tiempos, calidad de ejecución y resultados. - Coordinar agencias, partners y proveedores externos.
Contribuir a la mejora continua de la experiencia del cliente.
Licenciatura en Marketing, Administración, Comunicación, Ingeniería o afines.
+5 años de experiencia en marketing digital, growth, CRM o conversión digital. - Experiencia en gestión de proyectos y trabajo cross-functional.
Fuerte orientación a datos, pensamiento analítico y foco en resultados.
Experiencia en entornos dinámicos y orientados a performance.
description:
About the job

Estamos buscando un Analista de Marketing & Customer Experience para importante empresa multinacional.




Buscamos un/a Analista de Marketing & CX para México que actúe como nexo entre la

estrategia regional y la ejecución local. La persona será responsable de liderar iniciativas de adquisición, conversión digital, CRM y experiencia de cliente, trabajando de forma cercana con equipos regionales y locales para impulsar el crecimiento del negocio.




Esta posición tiene un fuerte foco en resultados, análisis de datos y ejecución, combinando visión

estratégica con capacidad de implementación en el día a día.




Responsabilidades principales: 

Liderar la planificación y ejecución de iniciativas de adquisición de usuarios y conversión digital.
Diseñar, implementar y optimizar campañas de CRM multicanal (email, push, WhatsApp y SMS).
Coordinar experimentos de growth y pruebas de nuevas palancas de crecimiento.
Asegurar la alineación de todas las iniciativas con los OKRs del negocio.
Trabajar de forma cross-functional con equipos de Marketing, Data, Producto, Tecnología y Atención al Cliente.
Analizar métricas clave, detectar oportunidades y optimizar continuamente el performance. - Gestionar proyectos asegurando tiempos, calidad de ejecución y resultados. - Coordinar agencias, partners y proveedores externos.
Contribuir a la mejora continua de la experiencia del cliente.




Requisitos:

Licenciatura en Marketing, Administración, Comunicación, Ingeniería o afines.
+5 años de experiencia en marketing digital, growth, CRM o conversión digital. - Experiencia en gestión de proyectos y trabajo cross-functional.
Fuerte orientación a datos, pensamiento analítico y foco en resultados.
Experiencia en entornos dinámicos y orientados a performance.

title: Corporate Trainer (Remote)
skills: Train & Coach: Guide Executive Assistants so they’re ready to be staffed with our customers through live sessions, simulations, role-plays, and 1:1 coaching.
Instructional Design: Build and update learning resources including curricula, rubrics, playbooks, and other materials so they're clear, practical, and true to how Viva works.

Stakeholder & Operational Coordination: Partner closely with internal teams to ensure EAs are ready to be matched with customers, successfully staffed, and performing at a high level by managing a high volume of cross-functional communication with good judgment and follow-through.

AI & Automation: Use AI daily to work smarter, coach EAs on practical AI adoption from basics to advanced use to offer the most AI-savvy EAs to our customers, and implement automations that streamline workflows and scale learning.
2+ years in training, instructional design, L&D, coaching, or similar roles
C2 English, written, spoken, and very engaging
Hands-on experience with AI/automation tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Zapier, LMS platforms, or similar); a fast learner, resourceful, and eager to stay up to date.
Experience using data to surface insights and drive improvement
Proactive, structured, and high-ownership by default, not by request.
People Developer: You know how to coach, motivate, and give feedback that sticks.
Strong Communicator: Clear, confident, and great at guiding people through ambiguity.
Driver Mindset: You take initiative and find ways to improve systems.
Scrappy: You’re resourceful, proactive, and thrive in fast-paced, changing environments.
Culture Fit: You believe in Viva’s mission and want to build something meaningful.
Growth & well‑being culture: Professional development programs, wellness initiatives, and empowerment practices built into daily work.
Remote setup: 100% remote roles, permanently.
Fixed schedules: Standard working hours, no overtime, weekends off.
Pay progression: Competitive compensation with reviews every 6 months and performance‑based bonuses.
Global health coverage: Medical, dental, vision, mental health, and routine care included!
Time‑off policies: Paid holidays, vacation, sick leave, and family care days.
Family benefits: Parental leave and flexible policies to support caregivers
description:
About the job

If you're energized by developing people, passionate about designing learning experiences, and believe AI should be embedded in what you do, Viva is the place where you, as a Trainer, will make the greatest impact.




At Viva, we connect fast‑growing startups in the U.S. and Canada with world‑class Executive Assistants (EAs) from Latin America. Our mission: expand career opportunities for women while hiring regardless of gender. The impact is intentional: when women thrive, families and communities do too. As a Trainer, you’ll train and coach our talent to succeed with the customers we partner with.




We post this role under different titles such as Trainer or Training Specialist; we want to find you, whatever your background looks like.




This is a full-time remote role based anywhere in Mexico, Guatemala, El Salvador, Honduras, Nicaragua, Costa Rica, Colombia, or Ecuador.




What you'll do:




Train & Coach: Guide Executive Assistants so they’re ready to be staffed with our customers through live sessions, simulations, role-plays, and 1:1 coaching.
Instructional Design: Build and update learning resources including curricula, rubrics, playbooks, and other materials so they're clear, practical, and true to how Viva works.
Stakeholder & Operational Coordination: Partner closely with internal teams to ensure EAs are ready to be matched with customers, successfully staffed, and performing at a high level by managing a high volume of cross-functional communication with good judgment and follow-through.
AI & Automation: Use AI daily to work smarter, coach EAs on practical AI adoption from basics to advanced use to offer the most AI-savvy EAs to our customers, and implement automations that streamline workflows and scale learning.




What we're looking for:

2+ years in training, instructional design, L&D, coaching, or similar roles
C2 English, written, spoken, and very engaging
Hands-on experience with AI/automation tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Zapier, LMS platforms, or similar); a fast learner, resourceful, and eager to stay up to date.
Experience using data to surface insights and drive improvement
Proactive, structured, and high-ownership by default, not by request.




What Sets You Apart

People Developer: You know how to coach, motivate, and give feedback that sticks.
Strong Communicator: Clear, confident, and great at guiding people through ambiguity.
Driver Mindset: You take initiative and find ways to improve systems.
Scrappy: You’re resourceful, proactive, and thrive in fast-paced, changing environments.
Culture Fit: You believe in Viva’s mission and want to build something meaningful.




💜 How We Take Care of You!

Growth & well‑being culture: Professional development programs, wellness initiatives, and empowerment practices built into daily work.
Remote setup: 100% remote roles, permanently.
Fixed schedules: Standard working hours, no overtime, weekends off.
Pay progression: Competitive compensation with reviews every 6 months and performance‑based bonuses.
Global health coverage: Medical, dental, vision, mental health, and routine care included!
Time‑off policies: Paid holidays, vacation, sick leave, and family care days.
Family benefits: Parental leave and flexible policies to support caregivers




Apply!

🕐 Ready to start ASAP? So are we. Move fast → get hired fast.

🔎 Important: All Viva communication comes from an @execviva.com email. Report any unofficial messages (e.g., Instagram, Facebook) to careers@execviva.com. We never request payments or personal information.

title: Content Creator
description:
About the job

Content Creator

Compensation: $3,000-$3,500 USD Per Month!




About Remote Leverage

Remote Leverage is a leading recruiting agency based in California, specializing in outsourcing and offshoring talent from Latin America and worldwide for U.S.-based businesses. You will be responsible for creating, posting, and analyzing video content across Remote Leverage’s social media platforms to drive engagement, leads, and revenue.




Responsibilities:

• Create engaging short-form and long-form video content for platforms including Instagram, YouTube Shorts, Facebook, LinkedIn, and TikTok.

• Write scripts and develop creative content ideas.

• Record and edit high-quality short-form videos that capture audience interest.

• Create and manage content calendars to ensure timely posting and engagement.

• Post content across various social media platforms and monitor audience interaction.

• Track content performance metrics to identify what drives engagement, leads, and revenue.

• Test and experiment with new content formats, hooks, and calls to action to optimize results.




Preferred Skills & Tools:

• Experience creating short-form and long-form videos for Instagram Reels, TikTok, YouTube, Facebook, and LinkedIn.

• Strong scriptwriting skills, including hooks, captions, calls to action, and video outlines.

• Ability to create and manage a social media content calendar.

• Comfortable analyzing content performance using Instagram Insights, TikTok Analytics, YouTube Studio, LinkedIn Analytics, or Meta Business Suite.




Qualifications:

• 2+ Years as a Content Creator or similar position

• Fluent English, both verbal and written.

• Stable internet connection, laptop, and headset.

• High level of motivation and persistence.

• Energetic and upbeat.

• Team player.

• Willing to listen to feedback and improve.




Interested? Hiring immediately. Apply now!

title: Video Editor
description:
About the job

About Zenit Corp

Zenit Corp is a rapidly growing ecommerce group that owns and operates multiple direct-to-consumer brands across various international markets.

Our focus is on building category-leading brands with strong fundamentals, scalable systems, and long-term growth potential. We are not building a single ecommerce business, but a portfolio of brands designed to scale aggressively over the coming years.

We are looking for creative individuals who want more than just a job. We want people who are motivated to create, execute, improve, and grow alongside the company.

The Video Editor will be responsible for producing high-quality video content that strengthens our brands, improves audience engagement, and supports the growth of our ecommerce businesses across multiple platforms.

This role is ideal for someone creative, fast-paced, detail-oriented, and comfortable working with modern creative tools, including AI-assisted workflows, to produce better content in less time.

Role

The Video Editor is responsible for editing and producing video content across social media, marketing campaigns, ecommerce content, brand materials, and internal projects.

This role requires strong editing skills, creative judgment, understanding of platform-specific content formats, and basic experience with AI-assisted content creation tools.

The editor will collaborate closely with marketing, creative, content, and design teams to deliver videos that are engaging, brand-aligned, and optimized for each platform and audience.

Video Editor Responsibilities❖ Video Editing

➢ Edit short-form and long-form video content for social media, advertising campaigns, ecommerce assets, and internal use.

 ➢ Add motion graphics, transitions, subtitles, sound design, music, and visual enhancements when required.

 ➢ Transform raw footage, product material, AI-generated assets, and creative briefs into polished video content.

 ➢ Ensure all videos are visually engaging, well-paced, and aligned with brand identity.

❖ AI Content Creation

➢ Use AI tools to support video production, editing, enhancement, and optimization.

 ➢ Understand basic AI workflows including AI-generated visuals, voiceovers, captions, avatars, and editing tools.

 ➢ Identify opportunities to integrate AI to improve speed, scalability, and creative output.

❖ Content Optimization

➢ Optimize storytelling, hooks, pacing, structure, and formatting to improve audience retention.

 ➢ Produce multiple variations of video content for testing, campaigns, and different platforms.

❖ Creative Collaboration

➢ Work closely with the creative strategist and marketing team.

 ➢ Translate campaign objectives into engaging video content.

 ➢ Apply feedback quickly and accurately while maintaining quality.

 ➢ Manage multiple editing projects simultaneously while meeting deadlines.

Role Objectives❖ Content Quality & Performance

➢ Produce high-quality, engaging video content across all platforms.

 ➢ Increase audience engagement and brand visibility through strong creative execution.

 ➢ Support marketing campaigns, product launches, and brand growth initiatives.

 ➢ Improve production efficiency through streamlined workflows and AI-assisted tools.

Requirements❖ Experience & Technical Skills

➢ Minimum of 2 years of experience in video editing or similar creative roles.

 ➢ Advanced proficiency in Adobe Premiere Pro, After Effects, CapCut, or similar tools.

 ➢ Basic experience with AI content creation tools and workflows (mandatory).

 ➢ Strong portfolio demonstrating previous video editing work.

 ➢ Solid understanding of social media content, pacing, storytelling, and retention principles.

❖ Communication & Work Style

➢ B2 English level or higher.

 ➢ Strong communication skills in both English and Spanish.

 ➢ High attention to detail and strong creative sensibility.

 ➢ Ability to work independently and manage multiple projects at once.

 ➢ Strong organizational skills and clear communication.

What We Offer❖ Compensation & Growth

➢ Salary up to USD 10,000 per year.

 ➢ High-performance, fast-paced work environment.

 ➢ Direct impact on a rapidly scaling ecommerce group.

 ➢ Long-term collaboration opportunities.

 ➢ Opportunity to create content for multiple ecommerce brands.

 ➢ Exposure to modern creative tools, AI workflows, and performance-driven video production.

Required Equipment❖ Work Setup

➢ High-performance computer.

 ➢ Stable and reliable internet connection.

 ➢ Quiet, distraction-free workspace.

Candidate Profile❖ Ideal Candidate

➢ Creative, fast, structured, and reliable.

 ➢ Strong visual judgment and understanding of engaging content.

 ➢ Comfortable using AI tools to accelerate content creation.

 ➢ Able to manage multiple projects, deadlines, and feedback cycles.

 ➢ Ownership mindset with consistent delivery of polished video content.

title: Head Of Strategic Expansion & Infrastructure Evaluation
skills: Strategic and analytical capabilities to assess new markets, evaluate infrastructure options, and build robust business cases for expansion.
Experience in project evaluation, including financial modeling, risk assessment, and scenario analysis for large-scale energy or infrastructure projects.
Background in infrastructure, energy, sustainability, or related fields, with familiarity in renewable energy, CCUS, or broader decarbonization initiatives.
Stakeholder management and negotiation skills to collaborate with public agencies, private partners, communities, and internal cross-functional teams.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex findings in a clear, concise manner to senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Proven leadership experience in building and guiding teams, setting priorities, and managing multiple concurrent initiatives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, economics, business, public policy, or a related discipline; a relevant graduate degree (e.g., MBA, MS, MPP) is preferred.
Professional experience working in or with Latin American markets, ideally including Mexico, and comfort operating in a multicultural, multilingual context.
Commitment to climate action, environmental justice, and equitable infrastructure development, with a track record of integrating these principles into decision-making.
description:
About the job

Company Description Terracause is a climate-focused organization committed to accelerating the transition to sustainable energy and infrastructure solutions. Building on expertise in renewable energy and carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), the company develops projects that support a decarbonized future and more resilient communities. Terracause collaborates with industry leaders, research institutions, and value chain partners to deliver high-impact, scalable solutions that reduce emissions and advance environmental justice. The organization is driven by a mission to set new standards in sustainability while creating long-term value for stakeholders and the planet.

Role Description The Head of Strategic Expansion & Infrastructure Evaluation is a full-time, on-site role based in Mexico City. This role is responsible for identifying, assessing, and prioritizing new markets and infrastructure opportunities that align with Terracause’s decarbonization and sustainability strategy. Day-to-day responsibilities include leading market and regulatory analyses, evaluating technical and economic feasibility of new infrastructure projects, and developing data-driven business cases for expansion. The role oversees due diligence activities, coordinates cross-functional input from technical, legal, financial, and policy teams, and prepares executive-ready recommendations for investment and partnership decisions. The Head of Strategic Expansion & Infrastructure Evaluation will also build and manage relationships with external stakeholders, including local authorities, infrastructure operators, community organizations, and potential partners, while establishing processes and metrics to track performance and risks across the expansion portfolio.

Qualifications

 

Strategic and analytical capabilities to assess new markets, evaluate infrastructure options, and build robust business cases for expansion.
Experience in project evaluation, including financial modeling, risk assessment, and scenario analysis for large-scale energy or infrastructure projects.
Background in infrastructure, energy, sustainability, or related fields, with familiarity in renewable energy, CCUS, or broader decarbonization initiatives.
Stakeholder management and negotiation skills to collaborate with public agencies, private partners, communities, and internal cross-functional teams.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to present complex findings in a clear, concise manner to senior leadership and non-technical audiences.
Proven leadership experience in building and guiding teams, setting priorities, and managing multiple concurrent initiatives in a fast-paced environment.
Bachelor’s degree in engineering, economics, business, public policy, or a related discipline; a relevant graduate degree (e.g., MBA, MS, MPP) is preferred.
Professional experience working in or with Latin American markets, ideally including Mexico, and comfort operating in a multicultural, multilingual context.
Commitment to climate action, environmental justice, and equitable infrastructure development, with a track record of integrating these principles into decision-making.

Stay up-to-date with industry trends and the competitive landscape in the LATAM market

5+ years of experience in sales or account management, preferably in the MarTech or SaaS industry
Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets
Strong understanding of customer engagement strategies and digital marketing
Excellent communication and presentation skills in Spanish, English and Portuguese
Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment

description:
About the job

At Braze, we have found our people. We’re a genuinely approachable, exceptionally kind, and intensely passionate crew.

We seek to ignite that passion by setting high standards, championing teamwork, and creating work-life harmony as we collectively navigate rapid growth on a global scale while striving for greater equity and opportunity – inside and outside our organization.

To flourish here, you must be prepared to set a high bar for yourself and those around you. There is always a way to contribute: Acting with autonomy, having accountability and being open to new perspectives are essential to our continued success.

Our deep curiosity to learn and our eagerness to share diverse passions with others gives us balance and injects a one-of-a-kind vibrancy into our culture.

If you are driven to solve exhilarating challenges and have a bias toward action in the face of change, you will be empowered to make a real impact here, with a sharp and passionate team at your back. If Braze sounds like a place where you can thrive, we can’t wait to meet you.

 

WHAT YOU'LL DO

As an Account Executive for the Central America LATAM region, you will be responsible for driving new business opportunities and managing relationships with key clients. You will work closely with marketing, product, and customer success teams to ensure that our clients achieve their engagement goals using the Braze platform.

Key responsibilities:

Identify and pursue new business opportunities within the Central America LATAM market
Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective and existing clients
Conduct product demonstrations and presentations to showcase the value of Braze’s solutions
Collaborate with the marketing team to develop targeted campaigns and strategies for the LATAM region
Negotiate contracts and close deals to meet or exceed sales targets
Provide feedback to the product team based on client needs and market trends
Stay up-to-date with industry trends and the competitive landscape in the LATAM market

WHO YOU ARE

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, or a related field
5+ years of experience in sales or account management, preferably in the MarTech or SaaS industry
Proven track record of meeting or exceeding sales targets
Strong understanding of customer engagement strategies and digital marketing
Excellent communication and presentation skills in Spanish, English and Portuguese
Ability to work independently and as part of a team in a fast-paced environment
Willingness to travel as needed within the Central America LATAM region
 

WHAT WE OFFER


Braze benefits vary by location, and we encourage you to review our specific benefits offerings for each country here. More details on benefits plans will be provided if you receive an offer of employment.

From offering comprehensive benefits to fostering hybrid ways of working, we’ve got you covered so you can prioritize work-life harmony. Braze offers benefits such as:

Competitive compensation that may include equity
Retirement and Employee Stock Purchase Plans
Flexible paid time off
Comprehensive benefit plans covering medical, dental, vision, life, and disability
Family services that include fertility benefits and equal paid parental leave
Professional development supported by formal career pathing, learning platforms, and a yearly learning stipend
A curated in-office employee experience, designed to foster community, team connections, and innovation
Opportunities to give back to your community, including an annual company-wide Volunteer Week and donation matching 
Employee Resource Groups that provide supportive communities within Braze
Collaborative, transparent, and fun culture recognized as a Great Place to Work®

ABOUT BRAZE

Braze is the leading customer engagement platform that empowers brands to Be Absolutely Engaging.™ Braze helps brands deliver great customer experiences that drive value both for consumers and for their businesses. Built on a foundation of composable intelligence, BrazeAI™ allows marketers to combine and activate AI agents, models, and features at every touchpoint throughout the Braze Customer Engagement Platform for smarter, faster, and more meaningful customer engagement. From cross-channel messaging and journey orchestration to Al-powered decisioning and optimization, Braze enables companies to turn action into interaction through autonomous, 1:1 personalized experiences.

The company has repeatedly been recognized as a Leader in marketing technology by industry analysts, and was voted a G2 “Best of Marketing and Digital Advertising Software Product” in 2025.

Braze was also named a 2025 Best Companies To Work For by U.S. News & World Report, a 2025 America’s Greatest Companies by Newsweek, and a 2025 Fortune Best Workplace in Technology™ by Great Place To Work®, among other accolades. Braze is also proudly certified as a Great Place to Work® in the U.S., the UK, Australia, and Singapore.

The company is headquartered in New York with offices in Austin, Berlin, Bucharest, Chicago, Dubai, Jakarta, London, Paris, San Francisco, São Paulo, Singapore, Seoul, Sydney and Tokyo.

BRAZE IS AN EQUAL OPPORTUNITY EMPLOYER

At Braze, we strive to create equitable growth and opportunities inside and outside the organization.

Building meaningful connections is at the heart of everything we do, and that includes our recruiting practices. We're committed to offering all candidates a fair, accessible, and inclusive experience – regardless of age, color, disability, gender identity, marital status, maternity, national origin, pregnancy, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation, or status as a protected veteran. When applying and interviewing with Braze, we want you to feel comfortable showcasing what makes you you.

We know that sometimes different circumstances can lead talented people to hesitate to apply for a role unless they meet 100% of the criteria. If this sounds familiar, we encourage you to apply, as we’d love to meet you.

Please see our Candidate Privacy Policy for more information on how Braze processes your personal information during the recruitment process and, if applicable based on your location, how you can exercise any privacy rights.

title: Part-time Technical Content Editor (B2B) - Remote Work
description:
About the job

At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley.




Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide.




When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success.




Part-time Technical Content Editor (B2B) at BairesDev




In this role, you'll collaborate with experienced SEO specialists, editors, product engineers, designers, and SMEs, working for a fast-growing unicorn in the software sector. Working with advanced SEO/AEO tools and AI systems, you'll help create and optimize content that drives search and AI discoverability. You will work on content for smart and skeptical technical audiences with no tolerance for fluff.

This is your opportunity to work at the intersection of technical content, SEO, and AI while contributing to the growth of a leading software company, with potential to evolve into a full-time content role in 2027.




What You'll Do:




Support our ongoing content update program.

Assist the SEO-on-site team in creating net-new content optimized for search and AI discoverability.

Collaborate on SEO experiments and product-related content.




What we are looking for:




3+ years working for North American-based tech companies

3+ years of technical content experience for B2B audiences.

SEO content experience and familiarity with AEO content.

Native-level English.

This is a part-time position, with potential to evolve into a full-time role.




Nice to have:




Experience working for a software development, software outsourcing, or consulting company.

Software background or experience with B2B software services or products.

5+ years of working for US-based B2B tech companies or enterprises.

Experience with AI tools, orchestration, workflows (Claude, Claude Code, ChatGPT, etc.).




How we make your work (and your life) easier:




100% remote - work from anywhere.

Excellent compensation in USD or your local currency if preferred.

Flexible hours - make your schedule.

Paid parental leave, vacation, & national holidays.

Innovative and multicultural work environment.

Collaborate and learn from the global Top 1% of talent in each area.

Supportive environment with mentorship, promotions, skill development, and diverse growth opportunities.




Join a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive and make a significant impact!




Apply now!

title: Marketing Technologist, Data Enablement
skills: Play a key role in configuration, integration and operations of marketing systems including Marketing Analytics Platform to help with automating data ingestion pipelines, building taxonomy tools, datasets for end to end Marketing Campaign performance and reporting.

This role is a combination of hands-on-keyboards(Manage marketing systems integrations, data ingestion, and built ETLs to prepare analytics datasets) AND program management (work with various teams to align on roadmaps for collecting, testing and preparing both third party and own data).

Integral team member driving the marketing technology roadmap by participating in onboarding of new platforms from implementation, integration, execution, data and security compliance review and training user teams.
Drive the shift to an AI-native operating model by applying systems thinking to fundamentally change workflows, leveraging AI as a 'creator' and 'true coworker'.

Lead the investigation and implementation of AI-driven tooling and integrations (e.g., Meta Ads AI Connectors, LLM-powered agents, Databricks embedded prompts) to automate campaign setup, content generation, and operational execution for marketing initiatives.
Enable self-service data and conversational analytics by deploying AI/LLM agents to allow non-technical teams to query diverse data sources and surface campaign performance insights.
Build dashboards/reports needed by marketing teams using AI and Genie spaces.
Coordinating with other dev dependencies to align cross-tech team sprint schedules & release plans.
Documents and maintains standard/efficient processes, automating as much as possible.
Enable cross-team testing scenarios.
You have 3+ years of experience in Engineering, Analytics, Tech. Operations, Marketing Technologist role, shipping scalable platforms and services in marketing-related domains
You are experienced in SQL, Python (or other programming languages), Databricks/Tableau Dashboards and familiar with ETL tools and integration with Marketing systems.
You have hands-on experience in using AWS services like S3, Lambda, etc.
You have experience applying systems thinking to drive the shift to an AI-native operating model and fundamentally change workflows.
You are skilled in implementing AI-driven tooling and integrations.
You have experience enabling self-service and conversational analytics by deploying AI/LLM agents.
You champion fast experimentation cycles with AI capabilities to validate and translate new technical direction.
You gravitate toward the detail but can keep the big picture in focus.
You love data and all that it can power.
You take a highly analytical approach, using data to drive step-change results.
You have experience in a TPM tool like Wrike or JIRA.
You exhibit a collaborative working style with a solid focus on execution
You have the modesty to question assumptions, seek broad input, and validate iteratively.
You have operational excellence, clear communication and take ownership of your work.
Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Operations Management, or similar field preferred or equivalent experience
description:
About the job

About The Team

We are looking for an experienced Marketing Technologist or Marketing TPM in the Marketing Operations space to help build highly coordinated and efficient infrastructure that enables Zillow's marketing organization. As part of this role, you will integrate with and operate on various marketing systems and will be a key point person for data, infrastructure, activation and analytics for marketing initiatives and campaigns.

About The Role

This role will serve as a technical liaison between marketing, analytics, engineering and marketing vendor teams to bring all partners together toward a common goal. The ideal candidate shines in a fast-paced and ambiguous environment, and has a balance of program management and hands on technical skills. If you like solving complex problems and are passionate about enabling marketing teams and delivering the vision of Zillow 2.0, we want to hear from you!


Play a key role in configuration, integration and operations of marketing systems including Marketing Analytics Platform to help with automating data ingestion pipelines, building taxonomy tools, datasets for end to end Marketing Campaign performance and reporting. 
This role is a combination of hands-on-keyboards(Manage marketing systems integrations, data ingestion, and built ETLs to prepare analytics datasets) AND program management (work with various teams to align on roadmaps for collecting, testing and preparing both third party and own data). 
Integral team member driving the marketing technology roadmap by participating in onboarding of new platforms from implementation, integration, execution, data and security compliance review and training user teams. 
Drive the shift to an AI-native operating model by applying systems thinking to fundamentally change workflows, leveraging AI as a 'creator' and 'true coworker'. 
Lead the investigation and implementation of AI-driven tooling and integrations (e.g., Meta Ads AI Connectors, LLM-powered agents, Databricks embedded prompts) to automate campaign setup, content generation, and operational execution for marketing initiatives. 
Enable self-service data and conversational analytics by deploying AI/LLM agents to allow non-technical teams to query diverse data sources and surface campaign performance insights. 
Build dashboards/reports needed by marketing teams using AI and Genie spaces. 
Coordinating with other dev dependencies to align cross-tech team sprint schedules & release plans. 
Documents and maintains standard/efficient processes, automating as much as possible. 
Enable cross-team testing scenarios. 


This role has been categorized as a teleworker position. Teleworkers do not have a permanent corporate office workplace and instead work from a physical location of their choice, which must be identified to the Company. Employees may live anywhere in Mexico, with availability to travel to Mexico City, as we recommend attendance at occasional office events.

In addition to a competitive base salary and benefits, this position is also eligible for equity awards based on factors such as experience, performance and location.

Who you are


You have 3+ years of experience in Engineering, Analytics, Tech. Operations, Marketing Technologist role, shipping scalable platforms and services in marketing-related domains
You are experienced in SQL, Python (or other programming languages), Databricks/Tableau Dashboards and familiar with ETL tools and integration with Marketing systems. 
You have hands-on experience in using AWS services like S3, Lambda, etc. 
You have experience applying systems thinking to drive the shift to an AI-native operating model and fundamentally change workflows. 
You are skilled in implementing AI-driven tooling and integrations. 
You have experience enabling self-service and conversational analytics by deploying AI/LLM agents. 
You champion fast experimentation cycles with AI capabilities to validate and translate new technical direction. 
You gravitate toward the detail but can keep the big picture in focus. 
You love data and all that it can power. 
You take a highly analytical approach, using data to drive step-change results. 
You have experience in a TPM tool like Wrike or JIRA. 
You exhibit a collaborative working style with a solid focus on execution
You have the modesty to question assumptions, seek broad input, and validate iteratively. 
You have operational excellence, clear communication and take ownership of your work. 
Bachelor’s Degree in Computer Science, Operations Management, or similar field preferred or equivalent experience


Get to know us

At Zillow, we’re reimagining how people move—through the real estate market and through their careers. As the most-visited real estate platform in the U.S., we help customers navigate buying, selling, financing and renting with greater ease and confidence. Whether you're working in tech, sales, operations, or design, you’ll be part of a company that's reshaping an industry and helping more people make home a reality.

Zillow is honored to be recognized among the best workplaces in the country. Zillow was named one of FORTUNE 100 Best Companies to Work For® in 2025, and included on the PEOPLE Companies That Care® 2025 list, reflecting our commitment to creating an innovative, inclusive, and engaging culture where employees are empowered to grow.

No matter where you sit in the organization, your work will help drive innovation, support our customers, and move the industry—and your career—forward, together.

Zillow Group is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive, innovative environment with the best employees. We are committed to equal employment opportunity regardless of race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, age, citizenship, marital status, disability, gender identity or Veteran status. If you have a disability or special need that requires accommodation, please contact your recruiter directly.

Qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment in accordance with applicable state and local law.

Los Angeles County applicants: Job duties for this position include: work safely and cooperatively with other employees, supervisors, and staff; adhere to standards of excellence despite stressful conditions; communicate effectively and respectfully with employees, supervisors, and staff to ensure exceptional customer service; and follow all federal, state, and local laws and Company policies. Criminal history may have a direct, adverse, and negative relationship with some of the material job duties of this position. These include the duties and responsibilities listed above, as well as the abilities to adhere to company policies, exercise sound judgment, effectively manage stress and work safely and respectfully with others, exhibit trustworthiness and professionalism, and safeguard business operations and the Company’s reputation. Pursuant to the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, we will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records.

description:
About the job

Descripción del puesto En el puesto de Especialista en CRM y automatización en Centro SanaMente te encargarás de diseñar, configurar y optimizar flujos de trabajo automatizados para la gestión de personas usuarias y pacientes potenciales. En tu día a día segmentarás bases de datos, crearás campañas automatizadas (emails, recordatorios, seguimientos) y monitorizarás su rendimiento para proponer mejoras continuas. Colaborarás con los equipos de marketing y atención al paciente para definir procesos, mejorar la experiencia de la persona usuaria y aumentar la conversión y fidelización. También documentarás los procesos, elaborarás informes periódicos y ayudarás a implementar nuevas herramientas o integraciones relacionadas con el CRM. Este es un puesto de media jornada (part time) y se desarrolla en modalidad remota.




Lo que necesitamos:

Configuración y optimización de flujos en WATI (WhatsApp Business API)
Diseño e implementación de secuencias de email marketing (Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign o similar)
Criterio para adaptar los mensajes a una audiencia que viene a buscar apoyo psicológico — no cualquier tono sirve aquí
Aptitudes analíticas: capacidad para interpretar datos, extraer conclusiones accionables y proponer optimizaciones basadas en métricas y KPIs.
Gestión de relaciones con personas usuarias y ventas: experiencia en Customer Relationship Management (CRM) y en procesos de ventas orientados a la fidelización y al acompañamiento personalizado.
Comunicación y trabajo colaborativo: excelentes habilidades de comunicación escrita y verbal, así como capacidad para coordinarte con equipos multidisciplinares de forma remota.
Gestión de proyectos: aptitudes en Project Management para planificar, priorizar y hacer seguimiento de iniciativas de automatización y mejora de procesos.




Requisitos no negociables:

Basado/a en México
Facturación activa (RFC con actividad económica vigente) — indispensable para formalizar la colaboración

Lo que ofrecemos:

Colaboración recurrente part-time con pago por honorarios
Autonomía real en las decisiones técnicas dentro de tu área




Si cumples los requisitos y te interesa, escríbeme por mensaje directo incluyendo: tu tarifa para la configuración inicial de WATI y tu esquema de cobro para secuencias de email (por proyecto, por hora o como lo manejes). Eso nos permite avanzar rápido.

description:
About the job

Who We Are

Solera is a global leader in data and software services that strives to transform every touchpoint of the vehicle lifecycle into a connected digital experience. In addition, we provide products and services to protect life’s other most important assets: our homes and digital identities. Today, Solera processes over 300 million digital transactions annually for approximately 235,000 partners and customers in more than 90 countries. Our 6,500 team members foster an uncommon, innovative culture and are dedicated to successfully bringing the future to bear today through cognitive answers, insights, algorithms and automation. For more information, please visit solera.com.

The Role

As an Inside Customer Success Rep you represent the face of the company and will primarily work with Large, Medium sized customers. You will build and maintain strong customer relationships with our customers while resolving customer issues. The Inside Customer Success Rep must be able to plan and execute daily and weekly, required activities. You must also be adept at multi-tasking, working within a team and other departments that drive successful business outcomes for the customer and for Solera-Omnitracs. In this role you must be comfortable using and/or learning new or other communication and automation tools. A great candidate does well with conflict resolution and can thrive in a fast-paced, change – heavy environment. The ideal candidate is goal and detail oriented, dedicated, hard-working, and maintains a positive attitude.

What You’ll Do


 Engage professionally with customers through multiple channels (inbound calls, outbound calls, email)
 Responsible for understanding and managing a subset of customer accounts
 Responsible for Customer Retention by fostering customer relationships and driving customer loyalty.
 Engage in active listening to promptly answer customer queries and concerns
 Work closely with the account manager to compose joint success plans with customers outlining their objectives, timelines and effectively removing any barriers to achieving business value
 Deeply understand the Omnitracs platform’s capabilities and explain them to businesses of all types – long haul transportation, last mite, ready-mix and more
 Manage customer cases to completion as escalated by the customer or internal Omnitracs teams
 Act as an escalation point for the Customer success front line of support
 Take impeccable notes and stay organized through the CRM to manage all accounts.
 Mitigate escalations and resolve customer issues.
 Guide users through navigating the company site or using products and services
 Be an advocate for the customer, be the voice of the customer
 Hold and lead recurrent customer business meetings
 Interact with other departments to resolve customer related matters
 Continued Development- Learn Industry News, ELD Mandates, Issues Related to Transportation and Updates to Omnitracs Products and Services.
 Ensure team goals, team KPIs, and deadlines are met
 Perform other duties and responsibilities as assigned


What You’ll Bring


 Experience working in a Customer Success environment
 Familiar with DOT/FMCSA/CSA Regulations a plus
 Results-oriented with strong problem- solving skills
 Exceptional customer facing customer skills, the ability to quickly understand customer needs
 Team-oriented and able to build and maintain strong inter-team and inter-departmental relationships
 Excellent verbal and written English communication skills
 Excellent presentation skills
 Excellent consultative skills
 Ability to adapt to and learn new applications and platforms
 Ability to work independently with minimal direction
 Ability to address and resolve customer issues independently
 Ability to prioritize projects and escalate issues to the appropriate parties as necessary
 Excellent organizational/multi-tasking and time management skills
 Intermediate to advance understanding of Salesforce, Five9 a plus
 Willingness to learn and use coaching feedback to improve.
 Strong Relationship (trust) Building skills
 French speaker a plus


Qualifications

EDUCATION: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent work experience and technical certifications

EXPERIENCE: 3+ years of experience in customer success, account management, sales, or consulting

It is impossible to list every requirement for, or responsibility of, any position. Similarly, we cannot identify all the skills a position may require since job responsibilities and the Company’s needs may change over time. Therefore, the above job description is not comprehensive or exhaustive. The Company reserves the right to adjust, add to or eliminate any aspect of the above description. The Company also retains the right to require all employees to undertake additional or different job responsibilities when necessary to meet business needs.

description:
About the job

Sobre nosotros

En Koltin, estamos rediseñando la forma en que cuidamos a nuestros papás, mamás, abuelos y abuelas, con el objetivo de ayudarles a mantenerse saludables, activos e independientes durante el mayor tiempo posible. Lo hacemos a través de nuestras membresías de salud, que integran:

Atención clínica enfocada en la prevención
Cobertura médica de gastos mayores
Programas de bienestar y comunidad

Somos la primera empresa en México que protege a personas adultas mayores, desde los 50 hasta los 84 años. Estamos creciendo rápidamente en todo el país, con un propósito claro: transformar la experiencia de salud para las Grandes Personas, haciéndola más humana, accesible y digna.




Sobre el Puesto

Koltin tiene miles de leads que ya conocen el producto, mostraron interés y no compraron. Ese backlog es el activo más eficiente del negocio — el costo de adquisición ya fue pagado. Hoy está subutilizado. Tu trabajo es convertirlo en ventas reales con un sistema data-driven.

Tu meta en septiembre: 860 leads del backlog convertidos en venta — el 56% del total de ventas de Koltin. El rol es 100% data-driven: cada decisión se toma con números. Si construís el sistema y lo hacés escalar, el rol crece con vos.




Responsabilidades

Diseñar la estrategia de reactivación del backlog: segmentación, scoring, mensajes y timing
Construir y mantener el modelo de conversión: analizar variables que predicen quién va a comprar y cuando
Definir las metas de conversión mensuales, medir el funnel end-to-end y reportar a la VP de Growth
Diseñar y optimizar flujos de CRM en HubSpot — automatizaciones, triggers, secuencias — con foco en D+30+
Explorar e implementar herramientas de AI para escalar el contacto sin perder personalización (AI SDR, scoring automatico, generación de mensajes)
Construir dashboards de conversión que le den visibilidad al equipo en tiempo real
Gestionar al ejecutor de CRM: asignar prioridades, revisar calidad de ejecución, dar feedback
Trabajar con el equipo de ventas para asegurar que los leads que pasan estén listos para cerrar
Construir el playbook de lifecycle — documentado y automatizado — que escale sin depender de vos




Eres un buen fit si eres una persona que tiene

3+ años en CRM, lifecycle o growth con foco en conversión
Experiencia construyendo flujos de nurturing y automatizaciones desde cero
Manejo avanzado de HubSpot u otra herramienta de CRM
Sólido en análisis de datos: sabes leer un funnel, identificar dónde se cae la conversión y actuar sobre eso
SQL o Python para análisis propio — no dependes de un analista para cada pregunta
Experiencia con herramientas de BI (Metabase, Looker, Power BI o similar)
Conocimiento o curiosidad real en AI aplicada a CRM: scoring automático, AI SDR, generación de mensajes
Experiencia liderando equipos pequeños o coordinando colaboradores
Mentalidad de dueño: te importa el resultado, no la actividad
Deseable: experiencia en salud, seguros o productos de alto ticket




Que te ofrecemos

Rol con impacto directo en el negocio — tus decisiones se ven en los números la semana siguiente
Equipo chico, decisión rápida, sin burocracia
Crecimiento real: este rol tiene proyección clara hacia liderazgo de toda el área de Lifecycle
Remoto 100% — trabajo asíncrono con reuniones sincrónicas focalizadas
Sueldo competitivo + variable por meta de conversión




Cómo medimos tu éxito

Leads del backlog convertidos en venta (meta mensual creciente)
Tasa de conversión D+30+ mes a mes
Calidad de los modelos y dashboards que construir — el equipo toma decisiones con tus datos
Adopción de automatizaciones y AI que escalan el contacto sin aumentar headcount
Autonomía y calidad del equipo que desarrollas




Beneficios residiendo en México

🎓 Acceso a herramientas y beneficios para tu crecimiento profesional, como cursos, talleres, libros, etc.
💊 Asistencia médica privada por videollamada (médico general, nutriólogo y psicólogo) ilimitado para ti y 3 familiares
🏈 Acceso a herramientas para tu cuidado físico (TotalPass)
🌴 9 días adicionales de personal leave por ley al año
👓 Seguro de Gastos Médicos Mayores
💻 Equipo necesario para hacer tu trabajo
💵 Apoyo mensual de $700 pesos adicionales al sueldo mensual
💎 Apoyo de 2,500 por ocasión única tras periodo de prueba

description:
About the job

🚨 IMPORTANT WATCH THIS QUICK LOOM VIDEO ON HOW TO GET HIRED

https://www.loom.com/share/345f776084e642ba99c24aabe40a67a0

C1 ENGLISH LEVEL IS REQUIRED. THE SELECTED CANDIDATE WILL BE SPEAKING STRICTLY IN ENGLISH DURING THEIR ENTIRE SHIFT.

PLEASE SUBMIT YOUR RESUME AND AUDIO RECORDING IN ENGLISH







🌟 Operations & Customer Support Specialist

🆔 Job ID: ALERIC1

🏢 Industry: Product Development, Manufacturing & Supply Chain Management

🌎 Location: LATAM (Spanish Required)

🕒 Job Status: Full Time

💰 Salary: $8 - $9 per hour

🚀 Target Start Date: ASAP







Role Overview 📌

About the Client

The client is a premier global product development, manufacturing, and supply chain management firm with over 25 years of experience delivering high-quality product solutions worldwide. While we specialize heavily as a top-tier manufacturer and distributor of air purification systems, our broader expertise lies in empowering businesses by streamlining the entire product lifecyle—from identifying the right global manufacturers and overseeing production to managing complex international shipping, importing, and final delivery.

About the Role

This isn’t a rigid, repetitive call-center gig. As our Operations and Customer Support Specialist, you will serve as the operational backbone of a lean, entrepreneurial team. You’ll be the warm, professional voice that US distributors trust over the phone and the detail-oriented partner managing daily financial transactions and orders in QuickBooks.

We are specifically looking for an active contributor, not a bystander. Because we operate as a tight team, you won’t just be following a script. We believe every team member should bring their brain to the table to help solve problems, build efficient processes, and directly impact our day-to-day operations. If you thrive in a collaborative, close-knit environment where your voice actually matters, you will love it here.

Key Responsibilities ✅

B2B Customer Service

Answer incoming phone calls from US-based distributors.
Assist distributors with order placement, product inquiries, and general support.
Build and maintain strong, professional relationships across our business network.

Operations & QuickBooks Management

Process and manage incoming orders accurately through QuickBooks.
Handle daily financial maintenance tasks, including processing payments.
Collaborate directly with ownership on a daily task list to keep business workflows moving smoothly.
Proactively look for ways to streamline workflows and suggest process improvements.

Reactive Digital Support

Perform basic, occasional website updates or maintenance if needed (no coding or development required).
Monitor inbound messages on social media channels (like Facebook) to ensure client inquiries are answered promptly. (Note: You will not be responsible for content creation, marketing, or running social media campaigns).

Qualifications & Skills 🎯

Experience

3–5+ years of experience in customer service, operations, or administrative support.

Bilingual Mastery

Flawless written and spoken English and Spanish (essential for communicating with our US customer base).

Tech Savvy

Proficient with QuickBooks (or similar accounting/ERP software) and comfortable picking up basic digital tools.

B2B Dynamics

Comfortable navigating business-to-business customer service and distributor environments.
