title: Especialista en diseño de productos y servicios
skills: Arquitectura de Nuevos Servicios (El Puente): Diseñarás el esqueleto operativo de nuevos productos, mapeando el Service Blueprint, la propuesta de valor y los flujos para que el SQUAD de desarrollo los haga realidad.
Prototipado Proactivo (Mentalidad Maker): Crearás aplicaciones ágiles, bots o automatizaciones en tiempo récord para validar ideas directamente en el mundo real sin depender de programación pesada.

Laboratorio de IA (El Explorador): Investigarás y dominarás activamente plataformas como Gemini, Claude y AppSheet para estructurar escenarios de negocio simulados y generar "cerebros digitales" que centralicen insights estratégicos.

Sinergia y Co-creación: Actuarás como el "Ingeniero de MVPs" apoyando al equipo de Experimentación a materializar sus pruebas científicas, y como el "Arquitecto de Inteligencia" automatizando la captura de datos para Innovación.
Pensamiento Estructural y Sistémico: Capacidad para descomponer un concepto complejo en sus piezas fundamentales (usuarios, datos, flujos de valor y tecnología).
Traductor de Negocio a Tecnología: Habilidad para escuchar una necesidad estratégica de la Gerencia y "traducirla" mentalmente en un conjunto de herramientas digitales.
Inclinación Natural al No-Code e IA: Alguien que use herramientas como AppSheet, Claude o Gemini en su día a día para resolver problemas y optimizar procesos de forma autónoma.
Resiliencia en la Experimentación: Que entienda que en el diseño de nuevos productos el fracaso es un dato valioso para iterar rápido y barato hacia el Product-Market Fit.
Por la funcionalidad e impacto real de los artefactos y prototipos creados.
Por la velocidad para transformar una idea abstracta en un MVP funcional.
Por el impacto final en la experiencia del cliente (NSS), participación de mercado y ventas de las soluciones que ayudes a diseñar.
description:
About the job

Especialista en Diseño de Nuevos Productos y Servicios 




La Misión de tu Rol: El Puente y Explorador Tecnológico

Tu misión será convertirte en el nexo vital de la innovación. Serás el Puente que conecta las ideas conceptuales con la realidad operativa, y nuestro Explorador Tecnológico, encargado de dominar herramientas No-Code y de IA Generativa para crear los artefactos y servicios del futuro.




¿Cuáles serán tus grandes retos y responsabilidades?




Arquitectura de Nuevos Servicios (El Puente): Diseñarás el esqueleto operativo de nuevos productos, mapeando el Service Blueprint, la propuesta de valor y los flujos para que el SQUAD de desarrollo los haga realidad.
Prototipado Proactivo (Mentalidad Maker): Crearás aplicaciones ágiles, bots o automatizaciones en tiempo récord para validar ideas directamente en el mundo real sin depender de programación pesada.
Laboratorio de IA (El Explorador): Investigarás y dominarás activamente plataformas como Gemini, Claude y AppSheet para estructurar escenarios de negocio simulados y generar "cerebros digitales" que centralicen insights estratégicos.
Sinergia y Co-creación: Actuarás como el "Ingeniero de MVPs" apoyando al equipo de Experimentación a materializar sus pruebas científicas, y como el "Arquitecto de Inteligencia" automatizando la captura de datos para Innovación.




¿Qué perfil y mentalidad buscamos?




Pensamiento Estructural y Sistémico: Capacidad para descomponer un concepto complejo en sus piezas fundamentales (usuarios, datos, flujos de valor y tecnología).
Traductor de Negocio a Tecnología: Habilidad para escuchar una necesidad estratégica de la Gerencia y "traducirla" mentalmente en un conjunto de herramientas digitales.
Inclinación Natural al No-Code e IA: Alguien que use herramientas como AppSheet, Claude o Gemini en su día a día para resolver problemas y optimizar procesos de forma autónoma.
Resiliencia en la Experimentación: Que entienda que en el diseño de nuevos productos el fracaso es un dato valioso para iterar rápido y barato hacia el Product-Market Fit.




¿Cómo mediremos tu éxito?




Por la funcionalidad e impacto real de los artefactos y prototipos creados.
Por la velocidad para transformar una idea abstracta en un MVP funcional.
Por el impacto final en la experiencia del cliente (NSS), participación de mercado y ventas de las soluciones que ayudes a diseñar.




¿Listo(a) para el reto?




Si tienes la mentalidad ágil que se requiere para crear el futuro de los servicios digitales y físicos, ¡queremos conocerte!

description:
About the job

If you’re looking for a career where you can make a real impression, join Global Service Center (GSC) HSBC and discover how valued you’ll be.

HSBC is one of the largest banking and financial services organizations in the world, with operations in 64 countries and territories. We aim to be where the growth is, enabling businesses to thrive and economies to prosper, and, ultimately, helping people to fulfil their hopes and realize their ambitions.

Client Service Specialist.

The commercial card service specialist team is a ring-fenced team aimed at replicating the client experience provided by card service managers. Whilst these service managers will not be geographically located in the same country as the client, they will provide the client expected service by leveraging technology. These will be service oriented individuals with the right amount of card knowledge to be able to address the different complex card queries from clients. They will not have a client portfolio, however, will own the query that they pick up from the generic pooled mailbox.

The Jobholder Is Primarily Responsible For


Ownership of card issues – Completely own card related issues. The service specialists will be the face of HSBC/GPS for card issues and liaise with the different departments where required. Card specialists will ensure constant updates and holding responses as appropriate.
First Contact Resolution – Whilst there will be instances where card service specialists will have to coordinate with different teams, the endeavour will always be to drive first contact resolution, either through email and or where required via call/zoom.
Creation of synergies between card operations, card service managers, client account manager, and contact centre.
Compliment / support card service manager and client account manager – The card service specialist will be complimenting the CSS and CAMs for card related issues as appropriate.
Connectivity – The jobholder will where possible engage the client through zoom video calls and not just through audio / emails.


Experience


Basic Portuguese and English language is necesary - speaking and writing
Fairly good understanding of payments, and commercial cards.
A passion for serving clients and managing clients, front line client engagement experience
Ability to own issues, to be able to be in front of a client (via Video) and take ownership
High commitment to service excellence and client relationship management
Strong knowledge of back end operational process
Strong interpersonal, influencing and communication skills
Experience in customer service in a front-line role, including complex query handling.


Due to the urgent hiring need, candidates with immediate right to work locally and no relocation need will be prioritized.

At HSBC we offer our colleagues a greater number of days, so that they can fully enjoy their wedding, take care of the new member of the family, or grieve the loss of a family member. Our paid leave package is at the forefront in Mexico, now you have one more reason to be HSBC and proudly live a culture of well-being, balance and care.

HSBC is an equal opportunity employer committed to building a culture where all employees are valued, respected and opinions count. We take pride in providing a workplace that fosters continuous professional development, flexible working and, opportunities to grow within an inclusive and diverse environment. We encourage applications from all suitably qualified persons irrespective of, but not limited to, their gender or genetic information, sexual orientation, ethnicity, religion, social status, medical care leave requirements, political affiliation, people with disabilities, color, national origin, veteran status, etc., We consider all applications based on merit and suitability to the role.

Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.

***Issued By HSBC Electronic Data Process Mexico Private LTD***

description:
About the job

Location:

San Andres Cholula, PUE, MX, 72830

Syntax is a leading Managed Cloud Provider for Mission Critical Enterprise Applications and has been providing comprehensive technology solutions to businesses of all sizes since 1972. Syntax has undisputed strength to implement and manage ERP deployments (Oracle, SAP) in a secure and resilient private, public or hybrid cloud. With strong technical and functional consulting services, and world-class monitoring and automation, Syntax serves some of North America’s largest corporations across a diverse range of industries. Syntax has offices worldwide, and partners with Oracle, SAP, AWS, Microsoft, IBM and other global technology leaders.

Customer Support Specialist

Position Summary

The Customer Support Specialist is responsible for providing exceptional support to customers by addressing inquiries, resolving issues, and ensuring a positive customer experience. This role serves as a primary point of contact for customers and works closely with internal teams to deliver timely and effective solutions.

Key Responsibilities


Respond to customer inquiries via phone, email, chat, and support ticketing systems.

Document customer interactions, issues, and resolutions accurately.
Escalate complex issues to appropriate internal teams when necessary.
Monitor and manage support tickets to ensure service level agreements (SLAs) are met.
Educate customers on products, services, features, and best practices.
Identify recurring issues and provide feedback to improve products, services, and processes.
Maintain a high level of customer satisfaction and professionalism in all interactions.
Collaborate with cross-functional teams including Sales, Operations, Product, and Technical Support.
Contribute to the development and maintenance of knowledge base articles and support documentation.


Required Qualifications


Bachelor's degree or equivalent work experience.
1–3 years of experience in Customer Service, Customer Support, Help Desk, or a related role.

Bilingual skills (English required; additional languages are a plus).

Why Syntax?

Become a part of our success story and work in a company with exciting innovation projects that are causing a stir across the industry. We recently launched one of the world's most advanced manufacturing facilities based on SAP S/4HANA Cloud and SAP Digital Manufacturing Cloud for Execution - for Smart Press Shop, a pioneering joint venture between Porsche and forming specialist Schuler.


Competitive, above-average compensation
Global tourist: With us, you can also work from abroad from time to time
Flexible working time models, home office
Attractive benefits, e.g. company pension scheme or various health offers
A modern environment in which the "you" is part of it
Open feedback culture, flat hierarchies and a motivated team
Individual career planning with continuous training and coaching on the job


You see a personal challenge in this responsible task? Apply now - and become part of the SYNTAX team!

title: SAP Invoice Resolution Coordinator
skills: Monitor SAP invoice hold reports to identify invoices requiring Buyer or Requisitioner action.
Review aged invoices, blocked invoices, GR/IR discrepancies, price variances, quantity variances, missing receipts, missing approvals, and PO-related issues.
Work directly with Buyers and Requisitioners to resolve invoice holds in a timely manner.
Coordinate with Accounts Payable to understand payment blocks, invoice status, and required corrective actions.
Validate purchase order accuracy, including pricing, quantities, delivery dates, supplier information, tax treatment, and payment terms.
Partner with Receiving and site teams to resolve goods receipt issues, missing receipts, and quantity discrepancies.
Support resolution of three-way match exceptions between purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices.
Escalate unresolved or high-risk invoice issues to Procurement, Finance, or site leadership as needed.
Maintain weekly reporting on aged invoices by Buyer, Requisitioner, supplier, site, and company code.
Track progress against invoice hold reduction targets and provide status updates to leadership.
Identify recurring invoice hold root causes and recommend process improvements.
Support supplier communications when payment delays are related to PO, receipt, or invoice discrepancies.
Ensure compliance with company purchasing, approval, delegation of authority, and AP process requirements.
Help develop standard work, training materials, and process documentation for invoice hold resolution.
Experience working in Procurement, Accounts Payable, Supply Chain, Finance, or Operations.
Working knowledge of SAP purchasing, receiving, invoice processing, or AP workflows.
Ability to review and interpret purchase orders, invoices, receipts, and supplier account details.
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to investigate issues and drive timely resolution.
Strong attention to detail and ability to manage large volumes of transactional data.
Ability to work cross-functionally with Buyers, Requisitioners, AP, Finance, and site teams.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to clearly explain required actions and follow up on open items.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including filtering, pivot tables, lookups, and basic reporting.
Prior experience with SAP invoice hold resolution, GR/IR clearing, blocked invoice reports, or AP aging reports.
Experience in a manufacturing, distribution, or supply chain environment.
Knowledge of three-way match processes.
Experience supporting supplier payment escalations or vendor statement reconciliations.
Ability to identify process gaps and recommend sustainable corrective actions.
description:
About the job

The SAP Invoice Hold Resolution Buyer / Requisitioner is responsible for supporting the timely resolution of blocked, aged, or disputed supplier invoices within SAP. This role works cross-functionally with Accounts Payable, Buyers, Requisitioners, Procurement, Receiving, Finance, and site stakeholders to identify root causes, clear invoice holds, and ensure suppliers are paid accurately and on time. This position is critical to improving supplier payment performance, reducing aged invoice backlog, maintaining strong supplier relationships, and supporting business continuity.

Key Responsibilities


Monitor SAP invoice hold reports to identify invoices requiring Buyer or Requisitioner action.
Review aged invoices, blocked invoices, GR/IR discrepancies, price variances, quantity variances, missing receipts, missing approvals, and PO-related issues.
Work directly with Buyers and Requisitioners to resolve invoice holds in a timely manner.
Coordinate with Accounts Payable to understand payment blocks, invoice status, and required corrective actions.
Validate purchase order accuracy, including pricing, quantities, delivery dates, supplier information, tax treatment, and payment terms.
Partner with Receiving and site teams to resolve goods receipt issues, missing receipts, and quantity discrepancies.
Support resolution of three-way match exceptions between purchase orders, goods receipts, and supplier invoices.
Escalate unresolved or high-risk invoice issues to Procurement, Finance, or site leadership as needed.
Maintain weekly reporting on aged invoices by Buyer, Requisitioner, supplier, site, and company code.
Track progress against invoice hold reduction targets and provide status updates to leadership.
Identify recurring invoice hold root causes and recommend process improvements.
Support supplier communications when payment delays are related to PO, receipt, or invoice discrepancies.
Ensure compliance with company purchasing, approval, delegation of authority, and AP process requirements.
Help develop standard work, training materials, and process documentation for invoice hold resolution.


Required Qualifications


Experience working in Procurement, Accounts Payable, Supply Chain, Finance, or Operations.
Working knowledge of SAP purchasing, receiving, invoice processing, or AP workflows.
Ability to review and interpret purchase orders, invoices, receipts, and supplier account details.
Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to investigate issues and drive timely resolution.
Strong attention to detail and ability to manage large volumes of transactional data.
Ability to work cross-functionally with Buyers, Requisitioners, AP, Finance, and site teams.
Strong communication skills, including the ability to clearly explain required actions and follow up on open items.
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including filtering, pivot tables, lookups, and basic reporting.


Preferred Qualifications


Prior experience with SAP invoice hold resolution, GR/IR clearing, blocked invoice reports, or AP aging reports.
Experience in a manufacturing, distribution, or supply chain environment.
Knowledge of three-way match processes.
Experience supporting supplier payment escalations or vendor statement reconciliations.
Ability to identify process gaps and recommend sustainable corrective actions.

title: Customer Service Wholesale
location: Cuajimalpa de Morelos, Mexico City, Mexico (Hybrid)
description:
About the job

¡Te invitamos a unirte a un equipo dinámico!




Somos una marca líder en el mundo deportivo que inspira a atletas y entusiastas a alcanzar su máximo potencial. Si compartes nuestra pasión por la excelencia, la innovación y el deseo de superar límites, esta es tu oportunidad para unirte a un equipo dinámico y lleno de energía.

 

Estamos buscando “CUSTOMER SERVICE JUNIOR PARA CANAL DE VENTA WHOLESALE”




 El objetivo principal del rol es garantizar una atención clara, oportuna y eficiente, fungiendo como el principal punto de contacto para resolver dudas, incidencias y requerimientos de las cuentas actuales de Wholesale, Sporting Goods y Tactical, así como de futuras cuentas que puedan integrarse al negocio.




Experiencia: Un año de experiencia en atención a clientes de venta mayoreo. Seguimiento a pedidos y devoluciones. Carga de órdenes de compra en portales, verificación de stock, coordinación de entregas, aclaración de facturas, solución a incidencias de retrasos. 




Conocimientos:

· Manejo de Excel intermedio-avanzado.

· Manejo de ERP (SAP, Oracle o similar).

· Conocimiento de procesos logísticos, inventarios y facturación.

· Manejo de portales de clientes y carga de órdenes de compra.

· Inglés avanzado.

 

Actividades:

Atención y servicio al cliente

· Dar seguimiento a pedidos, devoluciones, aclaraciones de facturación y solicitudes especiales.

· Mantener comunicación constante con clientes, tiendas y plataformas digitales, brindando información clara y actualizada.

· Asegurar una experiencia de servicio alineada con los estándares de la marca.

· Fungir como punto de contacto principal para resolver dudas e incidencias de las cuentas asignadas.

2.Gestión y seguimiento operativo

· Monitorear el flujo completo del pedido desde su colocación hasta la entrega.

· Resolver de manera proactiva incidencias relacionadas con retrasos logísticos, diferencias de inventario, cambios de producto y problemas de entrega.

· Dar seguimiento a órdenes pendientes y asegurar su cierre oportuno.

· Registrar en sistema toda la información relevante relacionada con pedidos, comunicaciones y soluciones implementadas.

· Cargar órdenes de compra en portales y sistemas internos.

· Realizar revisiones de inventario disponible y apoyar en la asignación de producto.

3.Coordinación interna

· Trabajar en conjunto con las áreas de Logística, Finanzas, Marketing, Ventas y Supply Chain para asegurar respuestas ágiles y efectivas.

· Escalar oportunamente riesgos o situaciones críticas que puedan impactar el nivel de servicio.

· Dar seguimiento a procesos de crédito, facturación y documentación requerida por los clientes.

4.Soporte comercial

· Apoyar al equipo comercial en actividades administrativas y operativas.

· Dar seguimiento a lanzamientos, promociones y proyectos especiales con clientes.

· Elaborar y actualizar reportes de ventas, inventarios y estatus de pedidos.

· Identificar oportunidades de mejora en procesos y proponer acciones para incrementar la eficiencia operativa.

· Contribuir al cumplimiento de los niveles de servicio (SLA) y los objetivos comerciales del canal.

 

Ofrecemos: 

Sueldo competitivo

Prestaciones Superiores y de ley

Fondo de ahorro 

Vales de despensa 

Vales de alimentos 

Seguro de Vida

Gastos médicos mayores 

Contratación directa por la empresa

 

Zona de trabajo: Santa Fe, modalidad de trabajo híbrido. 

Horario: de lunes a viernes de 07:00 a 16:00 o de 08:00 a 17:00 horas

About the Role

We are looking for fluent language professionals to help train and evaluate AI language models. This is a flexible, part-time contract role you can do entirely from home, on your own schedule. If you work with language professionally - as a writer, teacher, translator, tutor, linguist, or language specialist - this role is a strong fit.




What You'll Do

Read and evaluate AI-generated text for quality, accuracy, and fluency

Up to $7.50 USD/hr, paid weekly

Flexible hours - you set your own schedule
Part-time contract - ideal as a primary or supplemental income source

title: Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d)
skills: Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.
Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.
Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family.
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year.
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life.
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members.
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills.
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career.
description:
About the job

At Flix, we offer a dynamic work environment with competitive pay, strong growth opportunities, and a tech-driven approach to making travel more accessible, sustainable, and affordable

We are looking for a Senior Commercial Market Specialist (m/f/d) to join our North America commercial team. This is not a purely analytical role — we need someone who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to design and implement solutions, build bridges across stakeholders, and take ownership of results. You will interact directly with senior leadership, including the CEO for North America, and shape the commercial strategy of one of the world's fastest-growing mobility networks.

About The Role


Monitor and analyze regional performance, competitor dynamics, and demand patterns for the North America market, turning data into strategic recommendations.
Own fare management for the region: define regional pricing settings, fallback logic, and verify implementation across the network.
Support the design and execution of commercial activities — promotions, marketing campaigns, A/B tests, and sales initiatives — aligned with regional strategy.
Build and maintain a country-specific campaign calendar (email, social media, and other channels).
Develop and manage briefings for Marketing & Sales channels, including budget tracking and bi-weekly syncs.
Identify revenue and customer flow opportunities: social discounts, special products, customer segments, and willingness-to-pay insights.
Stay ahead of market trends: track the competitive landscape, macroeconomic shifts, regulatory changes, and network dynamics across the region.
Contribute to topline forecasts (weekly and monthly updates) and propose data-driven improvements.
Collect North America-specific e-commerce needs, build business cases, and present recommendations to leadership.
Lead or contribute to cross-functional projects, acting as subject matter expert or project manager on specific commercial topics.
Leverage AI tools and automation to streamline processes and improve commercial efficiency.


About You


Bachelor's degree in Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Business, or a related field.
2–4 years of experience in commercial analysis, revenue management, or business development — ideally in transportation, airlines, hospitality, beverages, or a similarly dynamic industry.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to go beyond the numbers: you design solutions, not just reports.
A natural connector who actively seeks input from stakeholders, builds relationships across teams, and is not afraid to knock on doors to get things done.
Strategic thinker who can navigate fast-changing macro environments (cost shifts, demand fluctuations, competitive moves) with adaptability and clarity.
Proactive and ownership-driven: you set your own goals, follow through on commitments, and hold yourself accountable to financial targets and project deliverables.
Curious about markets — you want to understand North America's geography, network, and customer dynamics, not just analyze spreadsheets.
Collaborative and a strong team player, with excellent communication and presentation skills for senior stakeholders.
Comfortable with ambiguity and freedom; you do not need constant guidance to deliver results.
Fluent in English (additional languages are a plus).
Interest or experience in AI-powered tools and process automation is a strong advantage.


We recognize that everyone carries a unique set of valuable skills and experiences. If you think you could have an impact even though you don't meet 100% of the requirements, we still encourage you to apply. We want to hear from you! 

What We Offer


Travel perks: 12 free Flix vouchers + 12 discount vouchers for friends & family. 
Work from (M)Anywhere: Depending on your role, work from another location for up to 60 days per year. 
Hybrid work model: We are an office-first company, but we offer flexibility to balance work and life. 
Wellbeing support: Access confidential 1:1 counselling, courses, and stress management for yourself and up to four family members. 
Learning & Development: Take advantage of language classes, training courses, and expert-led sessions to grow your skills. 
Mentoring Program: Connect with experienced colleagues to gain insights and accelerate your career. 


To view more local benefits specific to each office location, please check out this link: Locations - Flix Career

Why Join Flix?

At Flix, you’ll find teams that rally together to overcome challenges and spark creativity. We believe in ownership culture - giving you the freedom to take initiative, make an impact, and shape your own career path.

As we continue to expand across the globe, you can make a real difference in how we work.

If you’re ready to grow and lead your journey, Flix is the place for you!

title: Analista de Entrenamiento Operativo - Mercado Envíos
skills: Vibran energía emprendedora: se mueven por la curiosidad, nunca se rinden y se enfocan en superar sus propios límites.
Dan el máximo porque les gusta trabajar con compromiso y dedicación.
Viven los cambios como oportunidades y aprenden de sus errores.
La excelencia y la ejecución son claves en su forma de hacer las cosas.
Promueven el buen clima, aportan alegría y diversión.
Saben cómo construir con otras personas y disfrutan trabajando en equipo.
Garantizar la ejecución de la estrategia y programas de entrenamiento respecto a temas operativos y new features en el Centro de Distribución de Mercado Libre.
Generar acercamiento entre áreas para la ejecución de auditorías internas en procesos, a fin de implementar y asegurar mejoras en la operación.
Análisis de datos para la toma de decisiones en materia de entrenamiento para la operación de un almacén de productos voluminosos.
Organizar y ejecutar programas de capacitación relacionados a estrategias de mejora y calidad, y conducir y analizar las métricas e indicadores sobre efectividad de aprendizaje.

Dar soporte en la detección de necesidades de capacitación, entrenamiento y mejora continua en equipamiento de procesos de montacargas y colaborar en estrategias de change management entre los equipos.
Ser Ing. Industrial o profesional de carreras afines.
Habilidades transmisión de información y comunicación efectiva a nivel gerencial y operativo.
Generación de presentaciones a nivel profesional.
Manejo intermedio de bases de datos, tableros y Excel o Google Sheets.
Conocimiento de metodologías de Mejora Continua y Lean Six Sigma.(deseable)
Ser parte de una compañía con espíritu emprendedor en la que nos encanta pensar en grande y a largo plazo.
Ser protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje, crecimiento, expansión y proyectos desafiantes.
Compartir y aprender en equipo junto a grandes profesionales y especialistas.
Un excelente clima de trabajo, con todo lo necesario para que vivas una gran experiencia. :)
description:
About the job

En Mercado Libre estamos democratizando el comercio, el dinero y los pagos en América Latina.




En Mercado Envíos administramos el inventario de nuestros vendedores y entregamos los productos a nuestros compradores mejorando su experiencia en nuestra plataforma. En un mundo en constante evolución, nuestra capacidad para entregar rápidamente los productos que se compran a través de Mercado Libre se ha transformado en un requisito para competir. Forma parte del equipo que está transformando la logística en América Latina, ofreciendo soluciones personalizadas de clase mundial e integrando carriers locales y regionales, a través de un comercio que no reconoce fronteras




Tenemos un desafío para quienes:

Vibran energía emprendedora: se mueven por la curiosidad, nunca se rinden y se enfocan en superar sus propios límites.
Dan el máximo porque les gusta trabajar con compromiso y dedicación.
Viven los cambios como oportunidades y aprenden de sus errores.
La excelencia y la ejecución son claves en su forma de hacer las cosas.
Promueven el buen clima, aportan alegría y diversión.
Saben cómo construir con otras personas y disfrutan trabajando en equipo.




Imaginate emprendiendo proyectos desafiantes, dinámicos e innovadores y siendo responsable de:

Garantizar la ejecución de la estrategia y programas de entrenamiento respecto a temas operativos y new features en el Centro de Distribución de Mercado Libre.
Generar acercamiento entre áreas para la ejecución de auditorías internas en procesos, a fin de implementar y asegurar mejoras en la operación.
Análisis de datos para la toma de decisiones en materia de entrenamiento para la operación de un almacén de productos voluminosos.
Organizar y ejecutar programas de capacitación relacionados a estrategias de mejora y calidad, y conducir y analizar las métricas e indicadores sobre efectividad de aprendizaje.
Dar soporte en la detección de necesidades de capacitación, entrenamiento y mejora continua en equipamiento de procesos de montacargas y colaborar en estrategias de change management entre los equipos.




Requisitos:

Ser Ing. Industrial o profesional de carreras afines.
Habilidades transmisión de información y comunicación efectiva a nivel gerencial y operativo.
Generación de presentaciones a nivel profesional.
Manejo intermedio de bases de datos, tableros y Excel o Google Sheets.
Conocimiento de metodologías de Mejora Continua y Lean Six Sigma.(deseable)




Te proponemos:

Ser parte de una compañía con espíritu emprendedor en la que nos encanta pensar en grande y a largo plazo.
Ser protagonista de tu desarrollo en un ambiente de oportunidades, aprendizaje, crecimiento, expansión y proyectos desafiantes.
Compartir y aprender en equipo junto a grandes profesionales y especialistas.
Un excelente clima de trabajo, con todo lo necesario para que vivas una gran experiencia. :)

title: Content & SEO Specialist
skills: Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America.
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered.

Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority.
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited.

Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)
AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing
Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
Weekly Newsletter
Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)
Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
Template Pages & Gated Content
Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset
Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly
Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution
description:
About the job

Hi there! We are South and our client is looking for a Content & SEO Specialist!

Note To Applicants


Eligibility: This position is open to candidates residing in Latin America. 
Application Language: Please submit your CV in English. Applications submitted in other languages will not be considered. 
Professional Presentation: We encourage you to showcase your professional experience by including a Loom video in the application form. While this is optional, candidates who provide a video presentation will be given priority. 
Interview Policy: The use of artificial intelligence (AI) tools during interviews are strictly prohibited. 
Note: As part of the final stages of our hiring process, candidates may be asked to provide professional references for a reference check. If hired, you may also be asked to update your LinkedIn profile :)


The Core Job (Non-Negotiable)

These Four Areas Define Success In This Role


 AI-Assisted Blog Content → CMS Publishing


You will execute a structured content strategy that includes keyword research, content briefs, and a hub-and-spoke architecture already in place.

Your responsibility is to turn briefs into high-quality, published content.


Use AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, or similar) to generate first drafts from content briefs and keywords
Rewrite and refine AI output so it reads natural, human, and brand-consistent
Fact-check, improve clarity, and ensure editorial quality (no “AI-sounding” content)
Publish 2–4 blog posts per week into a CMS (proper formatting: H1/H2/H3, meta title, meta description, slug)
Source and integrate relevant visuals for every post (hero images, diagrams, screenshots where relevant)
Ensure images are contextually relevant, correctly sized, and include alt text
Add internal links following the hub-and-spoke structure
Implement FAQ schema markup where applicable (training provided)
Maintain tracking in the content calendar (status, publish date, URL, performance notes)
 Weekly Newsletter


Own a weekly email newsletter sent to a B2B audience (sales and revenue professionals).


Draft one newsletter per week in an email platform (e.g., Klaviyo or equivalent)
Curate 2–3 external insights (industry news, posts, tools, studies)
Write concise commentary and framing around each item
Include links to newly published content
Maintain a clear, non-promotional tone focused on value and relevance
Schedule, test, and send each issue
Monitor open and click-through rates weekly and flag performance trends
 LinkedIn Content (1–2 Posts Weekly)


Manage consistent organic presence on LinkedIn.


Write 1–2 posts per week for a company LinkedIn page
Avoid generic “corporate thought leadership” tone — keep it specific, practical, and grounded
Pull insights from blog content, product use cases, customer conversations, or sales insights
Create or source supporting visuals (screenshots, simple graphics, text-based visuals)
Schedule posts using a social scheduling tool or native platform
Monitor engagement and flag important comments or mentions for response
 Template Pages & Gated Content


Build and publish downloadable, conversion-focused content assets.


Write landing page copy for template downloads (based on provided briefs)
Format templates into usable assets (PDF, Slides, or Docs)
Publish gated landing pages in CMS with email capture integration
Ensure proper metadata, internal linking, and conversion flow
Maintain consistency between page copy and downloadable asset


Who This Role Is For

This role is for someone who is highly proficient in AI-assisted content production and can maintain quality at volume.

Required Skills


Strong experience using AI writing tools effectively (knows the difference between edited output and raw AI content)
Ability to write clearly, directly, and without corporate or filler language
Experience publishing content in a CMS environment
Strong organizational skills and ability to manage workload independently
Ability to source relevant, context-aware visuals
Comfortable receiving editorial feedback and iterating quickly


Strong Plus


Experience with CMS platforms (e.g., Webflow or similar)
Email marketing experience
Basic SEO understanding (metadata, structure, internal linking)
Experience writing for B2B audiences (sales, revenue, or SaaS context)
Familiarity with CRM ecosystems
Experience with LinkedIn content creation and distribution


Schedule: Monday to Friday, 9 AM – 6 PM ET

Compensation: USD salary

Location: 100% remote opportunity

If this opportunity sounds good to you, send us your resume!

title: RSNA Expert - Marketing Specialist
description:
About the job

Position Summary:

We are seeking an experienced Overseas Exhibition Project Manager to oversee the comprehensive management of the entire process for international exhibitions, including the Radiological Society of North America (RSNA) annual meeting. This role will lead exhibition planning, execution, vendor coordination, and client relationship management to ensure optimal brand presentation and participation outcomes at international events. Additionally, this position will serve as a key liaison for the company's digital accounts, collaborating closely with the marketing team to effectively convert offline exhibition traffic into online digital assets.




Position Responsibilities:

1. Exhibition Project Management (RSNA & Overseas Exhibitions)

· Manage the full lifecycle of planning and execution for the RSNA annual meeting and other overseas exhibitions, including booth reservation, exhibit material shipping, construction plan coordination, and on-site services.

· Maintain efficient communication with venues, external suppliers (construction, logistics, service providers) to ensure the smooth execution of all exhibition phases.

· Develop and manage project timelines and quality standards, ensuring critical milestones are met on schedule.

· Oversee on-site operations during exhibitions, including construction supervision, activity execution, and emergency handling.

· Possess thorough knowledge of RSNA rules and regulations to ensure exhibition behavior complies with organizer requirements and mitigates compliance risks.

2. Supplier and Budget Management

· Participate in the tendering, evaluation, and selection process for service suppliers (construction, logistics, PR, etc.), managing the Request for Proposals (RFP) process.

· Prepare and monitor exhibition project budgets, including booth fees, construction costs, shipping, travel expenses, etc., ensuring expenditures stay within budget and identifying cost-saving opportunities.

· Responsible for reviewing, archiving, and ensuring compliance for all exhibition-related contracts.

3. Client Relations and Sales Support

· Maintain efficient communication with exhibitors, providing professional services and on-site support.

· Nurture existing client relationships, identify potential client needs, and assist the sales team with registration and administrative processes.

· Collaborate with the digital marketing team to ensure sales leads generated during exhibitions are effectively entered into the CRM system (e.g., Salesforce) for data integration.

4. Cross-Cultural Communication & International Coordination

· Navigate cross-regional and cross-cultural work environments, building strong collaborative relationships with overseas organizers, service providers, and partners.

· Adapt to overseas travel requirements, leading teams to participate in RSNA and other international exhibitions, providing on-site support and problem-solving.

· Coordinate with internal departments (Marketing, Sales, Product, etc.) to ensure exhibition objectives align with the company's overall strategy.

5. Documentation and Reporting

· Organize, archive, and manage exhibition-related documents, including participation contracts, service agreements, and exhibition materials.

· Regularly prepare comprehensive post-exhibition summary reports, including effectiveness evaluation, ROI analysis, and recommendations for improvement

· Monitor industry trends and exhibition developments, providing data-driven insights and optimization suggestions for the company's exhibition strategy.




Required Skills & Education:

1. Education Background

· Bachelor's degree or above, preferably in International Business, Exhibition Management, Marketing, or a related field.

2. Work Experience

· Minimum 3 years of project management experience specifically with overseas exhibitions, having successfully led the preparation and execution of multiple international events.

· Experience with RSNA or other major medical/professional international exhibitions is highly preferred.

· Experience in domestic and international exhibition organization is preferred.

3. Professional Skills

· Proficient in the full cycle of exhibition project management, including planning, execution, and evaluation.

· Familiar with international trade practices and characteristics of overseas markets, possessing strong negotiation skills.

· Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite and project management tools (e.g., MS Project, Asana).

· Familiarity with CRM systems (e.g., Salesforce) is a plus.

· Experience in financial modeling and budget management is an advantage.

4. Language Ability

· Fluent in English with excellent listening, speaking, reading, and writing skills, capable of using English as a working language for business communication with international partners.

· Overseas work or study background is highly preferred.

5. Core Competencies

· Excellent communication, coordination, and execution skills, capable of building and maintaining strong collaborative relationships across departments and international teams.

· Strong project and time management skills, able to handle multiple tasks and emergencies simultaneously.

· High sense of responsibility and strong ability to work under pressure, adaptable to overseas travel and the high-intensity pace of exhibition work.

· Strong learning agility and market insight, capable of quickly grasping industry dynamics.

· Customer-oriented mindset with attention to detail and a pursuit of excellence in the industry’s exhibition experience.




Carestream is an Equal Opportunity Employer

Carestream is an equal opportunity organization. We recruit, employ, train, compensate, and promote withoutregard to race, religion, creed, color, national origin, age, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status,disability, veteran status, or any other basis protected by applicable federal, state or local law.

Applying for a job with Carestream

All applicants must complete the on-line application process.

description:

description:
About the job

About Puig




At Puig, we are a family-owned company with a long-term commitment to creativity, innovation, and people development. Through our Graduate Program, we are looking for exceptional young talent ready to begin their professional journey in a dynamic, international, and purpose-driven environment.




This is a unique opportunity to accelerate your career, gain exposure to a leading global organization, and contribute to the growth of prestigious dermatological brands that improve people's quality of life through science-backed skincare solutions.




Your Mission




As a Marketing Graduate (Entry Level) within our Derma Division, you will support the Marketing team in the development and execution of brand strategies, product launches, and commercial initiatives while gaining hands-on experience across multiple areas of the business.




You will work closely with cross-functional teams including Medical Affairs, Sales, Trade Marketing, Digital, and Finance, contributing to projects that have a direct impact on business performance and patient outcomes.




What You'll Do




• Support the planning, execution, and optimization of digital marketing campaigns across social media platforms, with a strong focus on TikTok and emerging digital channels.

• Assist in the creation, coordination, and publishing of engaging content aligned with brand strategies, consumer trends, and business objectives.

• Collaborate closely with the Digital & Marketing team to manage community engagement, monitor social conversations, and identify opportunities to increase brand relevance and reach.

• Support influencer and content creator initiatives, including campaign coordination, performance tracking, and reporting.

• Monitor digital KPIs and campaign performance, providing insights and recommendations to improve engagement, visibility, and business impact.

• Contribute to the development and management of a dedicated social media project aimed at healthcare professionals, helping create educational and scientifically relevant content for derma brands.

• Coordinate with Medical Visit and cross-functional teams to ensure content accuracy, compliance, and alignment with brand objectives.

• Assist in the preparation of presentations, reports, and business reviews related to digital performance, marketing initiatives, and key projects.

• Participate in local and global marketing projects, gaining exposure to brand management, consumer engagement, and omnichannel strategies.

• Provide support on ad hoc marketing initiatives, product launches, events, and special projects across the Derma division.




Who You Are

Recent graduate (Bachelor's degree completed within the last 1–2 years) in Marketing, Business Administration, Communication, Pharmaceutical Business, or related fields.
Previous internship, trainee, student leadership, or extracurricular experience is highly valued.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and presentation abilities.
Curious, proactive, and eager to learn.
Collaborative mindset and ability to work in diverse teams.
Passion for marketing, healthcare, skincare, and consumer behavior.
Comfortable working in a fast-paced and dynamic environment.




Requirements

Bachelor's degree completed by the start date.
Intermediate English proficiency (written and spoken).
Advanced knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel and PowerPoint preferred).
Availability to work full-time.
Authorization to work in Mexico.




What We Offer

Participation in a global Graduate Program designed to accelerate your development.
Exposure to senior leaders and international projects.
Structured learning and development opportunities.
Mentorship and career guidance.
A collaborative, inclusive, and innovative culture.
Opportunity to build a long-term career within Puig.




Our Commitment

At Puig, we believe that diversity, equity, and inclusion drive innovation and creativity. We are committed to creating an environment where everyone can thrive, contribute, and grow.

Join us and help shape the future of dermatological skincare through science, innovation, and purpose. ✨

We produce and distribute premier filmed entertainment and programming through our powerhouse film and television studios, including Universal Pictures, DreamWorks Animation, and Focus Features, and the four global television studios under the Universal Studio Group banner, and operate industry-leading theme parks and experiences around the world through Universal Destinations & Experiences, including Universal Orlando Resort, home to Universal Epic Universe, and Universal Studios Hollywood. NBCUniversal is a subsidiary of Comcast Corporation. Visit www.nbcuniversal.com for more information.



Our impact is rooted in improving the communities where our employees, customers, and audiences live and work. title: Market Researcher
skills: Support IDC’s market research agenda by conducting in-depth primary and secondary research into IT markets and their impact on industries.
Track, monitor, and identify IT industry developments to identify and report on market shifts, patterns, and anomalies.
Collaborate with internal subject matter expert analysts to achieve research goals and produce deliverables efficiently.
Participate in ongoing professional development and contribute to team knowledge sharing.
Synthesize raw data into information and meaningful insights and deliver tailored content components and assets that address key business requirements.
Contribute to the creation of written research studies and presentations summarizing findings for internal and external stakeholders.
Bachelor’s degree required; (business, economics, management information systems, technology marketing, product management or related field).
2-3 years of experience in market research, consulting, or analysis, ideally in the information technology sector,
Previous experience in roles such as market intelligence or competitive intelligence is preferred.
Outstanding written and strong verbal communication skills.
Proven ability to manage multiple assignments and meet tight deadlines.
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate with and across teams globally.
Experience with office productivity tools such as Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Some experience with any AI tools such as MS CoPilot, Google Gemini, Perplexity, ChatGPT, or other similar tools.

A strong interest in the IT market, including, but not limited in topics such as AI and data management, security, enterprise applications, enterprise infrastructure, devices, networking, telecommunications, application development, and services.
Understanding and interest in the application of information technology to industry use cases.
Flexibility to work both independently as well as collaboratively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Professional fluency in English.
Experience working with or for technology vendors, IT distributors, or in a market research/consulting firm.
Experience working in or analyzing emerging markets within the technology sector.
Knowledge of business models, go-to-market strategies, and technology adoption patterns in enterprise IT.
Intellectual curiosity, willingness to learn new marker research methods and tools, analytical mindset, and a collaborative, supportive approach to teamwork.
description:
About the job

Overview

About the Role & Team

IDC, a global leader in technology market intelligence, is seeking a market researcher to join our team . This role requires a mix of analytical abilities, attention to detail, proficiency in research methodologies, alongside excellent communication skills to convey complex information effectively. The market researcher will play a crucial role in interpreting data, staying abreast of IT market trends and events , supporting their team with in-depth research, and work ing collaboratively to meet research objectives. The market researcher will play a crucial role in supporting the authorship and delivery of IDC’s market intelligence products and services in a rapidly evolving information technology landscape. The role will focus on utilizing the latest market research methodologies and tools to uncover relevant data from internal and external data sources and synthesizing into market research content assets.

What You’ll Do


Support IDC’s market research agenda by conducting in-depth primary and secondary research into IT markets and their impact on industries.
Track, monitor, and identify IT industry developments to identify and report on market shifts, patterns, and anomalies.
Collaborate with internal subject matter expert analysts to achieve research goals and produce deliverables efficiently.
Participate in ongoing professional development and contribute to team knowledge sharing.
Synthesize raw data into information and meaningful insights and deliver tailored content components and assets that address key business requirements.
Contribute to the creation of written research studies and presentations summarizing findings for internal and external stakeholders.


What You Bring


Bachelor’s degree required; (business, economics, management information systems, technology marketing, product management or related field).
2-3 years of experience in market research, consulting, or analysis, ideally in the information technology sector,
Previous experience in roles such as market intelligence or competitive intelligence is preferred.
Outstanding written and strong verbal communication skills.
Proven ability to manage multiple assignments and meet tight deadlines.
Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to collaborate with and across teams globally.
Experience with office productivity tools such as Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.
Some experience with any AI tools such as MS CoPilot, Google Gemini, Perplexity, ChatGPT, or other similar tools.
A strong interest in the IT market, including, but not limited in topics such as AI and data management, security, enterprise applications, enterprise infrastructure, devices, networking, telecommunications, application development, and services.
Understanding and interest in the application of information technology to industry use cases.
Flexibility to work both independently as well as collaboratively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Professional fluency in English.


Preferred Attributes


Experience working with or for technology vendors, IT distributors, or in a market research/consulting firm.
Experience working in or analyzing emerging markets within the technology sector.
Knowledge of business models, go-to-market strategies, and technology adoption patterns in enterprise IT.
Intellectual curiosity, willingness to learn new marker research methods and tools, analytical mindset, and a collaborative, supportive approach to teamwork.


Why This Role Stands Out

At IDC, your work helps shape how the world understands technology and where it goes next. You collaborate with curious, high-caliber colleagues who value rigor, integrity, and shared success. As the premier global provider of trusted technology intelligence, IDC equips business and technology leaders with the evidence they need to make confident decisions. Our insights inform strategy, investment, and innovation across industries and regions.

Recognized by IIAR as Analyst Firm of the Year for five consecutive years, IDC sets the standard for credibility and impact. With more than 1,000 analysts worldwide and a truly global perspective, we combine deep expertise with practical relevance. Here, your ideas matter, your voice is heard, and your contributions provide the insights leaders rely on every day. It is meaningful work, backed by a culture that supports growth, collaboration, and long-term career development with a globally respected brand.

Equal Opportunity Employer

IDC is committed to providing equal employment opportunities for all qualified persons. Employment eligibility verification required. We participate in E-Verify.

About the Role

Read and evaluate AI-generated text for quality, accuracy, and fluency
Write short prompts and creative responses to help train AI systems
Rank and compare AI outputs based on clear evaluation criteria
Provide written feedback on language quality




What We're Looking For

Fluency in your language and English
Bachelor's degree or higher, preferably in a humanities, language, or writing-related field
Professional experience working with language - writing, teaching, translating, editing, annotating, or related fields
Strong attention to detail and ability to follow evaluation guidelines
Background in linguistics, academic writing, data annotation, or AI content evaluation is a plus




What You Get

Up to $7.50 USD/hr, paid weekly
Fully remote - work from anywhere
Flexible hours - you set your own schedule
Part-time contract - ideal as a primary or supplemental income source




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.

ROLE OVERVIEW




About the Client:

The client is a globally recognized author, executive coach, and keynote speaker specializing in leadership, mindset, and personal development. She works with high-level executives and organizations, delivering coaching, speaking engagements, and thought leadership content across multiple platforms. Her business is fast-paced and content-driven, with a strong focus on expanding her digital presence through social media, written content, and media appearances. She values professionalism, efficiency, and working with individuals who can think strategically, have a strong ‘marketing brain’ and can operate independently.




About the Role:

This role combines executive assistance with strategic social media and content support. The ideal candidate will manage light administrative tasks while primarily focusing on content creation, optimization, and digital growth initiatives as well as some communication with media, clients and prospect clients. This is not a task-based role. The client is looking for someone who can think critically, anticipate needs, and take initiative without constant direction. The role requires a highly proactive individual who can bring ideas, improve processes, and contribute to growing the client’s online presence. This role has potential to grow in scope and compensation for the right person.

Continuously propose ideas to increase engagement and visibility

Assist with visual content and simple graphic design needs

Strong experience with LinkedIn, Instagram, and Facebook

description:
About the job

I’m helping Email Clout find a top candidate to join their team flexible for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager.




You'll drive revenue and client success by independently managing end-to-end email marketing campaigns.




Compensation:

USD 300 - 1.5K/month




Location:

Remote: Mexico




Mission of Email Clout:

"Empower online brands with high-impact, revenue-driving email campaigns. Fast, accountable, and world-class results."




What makes you a strong candidate:

You are proficient in Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting.
You have the potential to develop in Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma.
English - Fully fluent




Responsibilities and more:

Email Clout is an email marketing agency that partners with online brands to create unique, engaging, and revenue-generating email campaigns and flows. We’re fully remote, small, and love to move fast. We hold ourselves accountable for delivering a world-class client experience and industry-shattering email performance. 




We are looking for the ideal candidate for the role of Email Marketing Account Manager, who will be responsible for the experience and performance of client accounts. In this role, you will manage and execute email marketing campaigns and flows from start to finish. This includes coming up with ideas, planning, writing copy, communicating with clients and internal teams, as well as implementation in Klaviyo. You will use Klaviyo and Figma while managing your tasks independently through Notion. Below are the key responsibilities for this role. 




- Create and manage multiple email content calendars. 

- Plan, write, and schedule email marketing campaigns and flows. 

- Collect, report, present, and analyze past data to make the best decisions for clients. 

- Conduct and lead client calls. 

- Handle difficult questions related to email performance. 

- Make design and copy changes in Figma. 

- Collaborate and manage designers and copywriters. 

- Stay up-to-date with industry trends and best practices in email marketing. 




Software stack you’ll be using: 

- Slack 

- Klaviyo 

- Figma 

- Shopify 

- Notion 




Requirements: 

- 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, preferably with experience using Klaviyo. 

- Proficiency in Figma or similar design software. 

- Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 

- Strong attention to detail and multitasking ability. 

- Familiarity with email marketing best practices and metrics. 

- Confident in communicating with clients. 

- Perfect English (spoken and written). 




Do Not Apply If: 

- You’re okay with delays or errors in client work. 

- You’re willing to work only during regular hours. 

- You can only write when assisted by ChatGPT. 

- You don’t take deadlines seriously. 

- You lack urgency in completing work. 

- You don’t have the resourcefulness to solve problems on your own first. 

- You don’t feel confident in your email marketing abilities.




Your potential leader(s):

Minh Vo - Founder



Desired Skills and Experience

Project management, Marketing strategy, Email marketing, Data analytics, Copywriting, Client communication, Slack, Shopify, Notion, Figma

description:
About the job

No Relocation Assistance Offered

Job Number #174335 - Del.Miguel Hidalgo, Mexico City, Mexico

Who We Are

Colgate-Palmolive Company is a global consumer products company operating in over 200 countries specialising in Oral Care, Personal Care, Home Care, Skin Care, and Pet Nutrition. Our products are trusted in more households than any other brand in the world, making us a household name!

Join Colgate-Palmolive, a caring, innovative growth company reimagining a healthier future for people, their pets, and our planet. Guided by our core values—Caring, Inclusive, and Courageous—we foster a culture that inspires our people to achieve common goals. Together, let's build a brighter, healthier future for all.

Role Summary: 

The Science Diet Brand Manager is responsible for the development and execution of marketing strategies across commercial operations to achieve the financial and market share objectives. They lead the go-to-market planning behind innovation launches, promotional activations, traditional and digital media and 6P strategies for omni-channel customers. They ensure consistent and effective brand messaging to the veterinary profession/ consumer, and consistent consideration of retail environment specific needs/tactics. They are critical in creating and driving the execution of revenue growth management and simplification opportunities for the brand.

Responsibilities:


Strategic Growth & Omni-Channel Execution: Align and implement omni-channel marketing, communication, and media strategies across traditional and digital platforms, using insights and analytics to capture key growth opportunities. 
Go-To-Market & Innovation Leadership: Lead the strategic planning, market alignment, and launch execution for new products, re-launches, and quarterly tentpole marketing activations. 
Strategic 6P Planning & Metrics: Develop and align measurable 6P marketing plans, identifying key performance indicators (KPIs) to track campaign effectiveness and business health. 
Portfolio & Revenue Management: Drive product portfolio management and identify revenue growth management and simplification opportunities across therapeutic nutrition categories. 
Financial & Budget Accountability: Manage the allocated A&P budget and support overall P&L management to maximize return on marketing investments. 
Commercial & Field Enablement: Design, produce, and hand over high-impact promotional materials to Sales Operations, ensuring field teams and veterinary conference strategies are fully equipped to win in the retail clinic environment. 


Required Qualifications:


Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Business Administration or a related field 
3+ years of experience in brand marketing, retail marketing, trade marketing or related fields
Proven experience executing digital and eCommerce marketing campaigns (including paid search, digital display, and social media)
Advanced English level


Preferred Qualifications:


Strong analytical skills and capability to uncover measurable insights in large datasets 
Excellent project management skills, with the ability to lead multiple projects simultaneously
Exceptional communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to work effectively in a team environment
Critical thinking and problem solving


Our Commitment to Inclusion

Our journey begins with our people—developing strong talent with diverse backgrounds and perspectives to best serve our consumers around the world and fostering an inclusive environment where everyone feels a true sense of belonging. We are dedicated to ensuring that each individual can be their authentic self, is treated with respect, and is empowered by leadership to contribute meaningfully to our business.

Equal Opportunity Employer

Colgate is an equal opportunity employer and all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, colour, religion, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, ethnicity, age, disability, marital status, veteran status (United States positions), or any other characteristic protected by law.

Reasonable accommodation during the application process is available for persons with disabilities. Please complete this request form should you require accommodation.

title: Marketing Operations Partner - Remote Work
description:
About the job

At BairesDev®, we've been leading the way in technology projects for over 15 years. We deliver cutting-edge solutions to giants like Google and the most innovative startups in Silicon Valley.




Our diverse 4,000+ team, composed of the world's Top 1% of tech talent, works remotely on roles that drive significant impact worldwide.




When you apply for this position, you're taking the first step in a process that goes beyond the ordinary. We aim to align your passions and skills with our vacancies, setting you on a path to exceptional career development and success.




Marketing Operations Partner at BairesDev




Marketing Operations Partner at BairesDev




In this role, you'll work directly with senior leadership, driving high-impact marketing and strategic initiatives that shape how the organization operates and grows. You'll have full visibility into the business, working across multiple teams and functions while playing a key role in ensuring alignment, efficiency, and execution excellence. This is a high-exposure, fast-learning position designed for someone ambitious, analytical, and proactive—someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, takes ownership, and wants to grow into a strategic leadership role within the marketing organization.




What You'll Do:




- Drive the execution and follow-up of cross-functional marketing projects, ensuring alignment, accountability, and timely delivery of key initiatives.

- Support the Head of Marketing Operations in strategic planning, special projects, and performance analysis, contributing to data-driven decision-making and operational excellence.

- Oversee the day-to-day operations of the Marketing Operations function, ensuring business continuity, leading selected initiatives, and maintaining a high-performance execution rhythm.

- Develop and optimize processes, dashboards, and reporting systems to improve efficiency, visibility, and collaboration across all marketing functions.

- Coordinate various internal stakeholders, including team members, internal clients, and senior marketing leadership to ensure smooth communication, prioritization, and project alignment.




What we are looking for:




- 2+ years of experience in fast-paced environments such as strategic consulting (MBB + boutique), fast-growing startups or scale-ups (growth, ops, or strategy), or top-tier multinational corporations (e.g., marketing, tech, or financial services) with a track record of agility, analytical rigor, and cross-functional impact.

- Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Economics, Industrial Engineering, Accounting, Marketing, or any field strongly connected to business management.

- Advanced proficiency in English.




How we do make your work (and your life) easier:




- 100% remote work (from anywhere).

- Excellent compensation in USD or your local currency if preferred

- Hardware and software setup for you to work from home.

- Flexible hours: create your own schedule.

- Paid parental leaves, vacations, and holidays.

- Innovative and multicultural work environment: collaborate and learn from the global Top 1% of talent.

- Supportive environment with mentorship, promotions, skill development, and diverse growth opportunities.




Apply now and become part of a global team where your unique talents can truly thrive!
